{"parentUuid":"13a7c741-28ec-4ad5-b315-0b46eb5f6937","isSidechain":false,"message":{"model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","id":"msg_01BcRGaLxvfwe8wfZcqBQisc","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Correct â€” and the good news is that Layers 1 and 2 require **zero training on CWQ data**. The distinction matters:\n\n**Training (what you want to avoid):** adjusting model weights on CWQ-specific examples â€” gradient descent, epochs, dataset-specific learning. TransE, GCN, fine-tuning all fall here.\n\n**On-the-fly inference (what CEREBRUM already does):** using general-purpose tools that were pre-built once and applied to any graph without modification.\n\nThe two that qualify:\n\n---\n\n### Layer 1 â€” Generalized CompositionRule (fully deterministic)\n\nNo model at all. Pure graph logic:\n\n```\nA â†’ [directed_by] â†’ B â†’ [born_in] â†’ C\nâŸ¹  infer  A â†’ [directed_by:born_in] â†’ C\n```\n\nScan every node B in the graph. For every in-edge (Aâ†’B) and out-edge (Bâ†’C), materialize the shortcut Aâ†’C. Two rounds extends 2-hop reach to 4-hop. This is arithmetic on edges â€” no weights, no training, runs in O(E Ã— avg_degree).\n\nThe `CompositionRule` in `graph_completion.py` already does this for named relation pairs. We just need a generalized wrapper that auto-enumerates all pairs.\n\n---\n\n### Layer 2 â€” IncompletenessRepairEngine (pre-trained general tool)\n\nUses `SentenceEngine` (all-MiniLM-L6-v2), which is like using a dictionary â€” it was built once by someone else, runs at inference time on any text, never touches CWQ data. The repair engine:\n\n1. Runs traversal, finds where paths die at low-degree frontier nodes\n2. Computes cosine similarity between the dead-end node's embedding (\"Capital of France\") and all nodes in other components\n3. Synthesizes the highest-similarity bridge edge\n\nSame principle as the CSA attention formula already in CEREBRUM â€” pre-trained embeddings used structurally, no QA-specific training.\n\n---\n\n### Layer 3 â€” Adamic-Adar (pure topology)\n\nNot even a model. Just counting shared neighbors:\n\n```\nscore(A, C) = Î£  1 / log(degree(B))\n              B âˆˆ common_neighbors(A, C)\n```\n\nOne NetworkX call, runs on-the-fly from graph structure alone.\n\n---\n\n**The principle**: CEREBRUM uses pre-trained general tools (SentenceEngine, structural encoders) as fixed inference components â€” the same way you'd use a ruler to measure without re-manufacturing the ruler for each job. None of these modify weights based on CWQ data. Want me to implement Layers 1+2 in `cwq_eval.py`?"}],"stop_reason":"end_turn","stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":3,"cache_creation_input_tokens":1848,"cache_read_input_tokens":159565,"output_tokens":1745,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":1848,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[],"speed":"standard"}},"requestId":"req_011CZdKjjGvoMB2mc25dxA9a","type":"assistant","uuid":"5b12008a-f959-4637-8f98-5890892159f6","timestamp":"2026-04-01T15:56:22.263Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"E:\\Development\\Cerebrum","sessionId":"1b39a2ba-e177-466c-ade0-cb0f0629b3b0","version":"2.1.87","gitBranch":"main","slug":"dynamic-marinating-meadow"}

{"parentUuid":"fe2e57cc-9f5a-4e24-809b-2c59a4f76d59","isSidechain":false,"message":{"model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","id":"msg_016Hb6V5p4jXcUzTY239BaWB","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Yes â€” and this is one of the strongest architectural properties of CEREBRUM. Let me be precise about why.\n\n**Crystal-box** = every inference step is not just observable but *logically verifiable*. No statistical opacity anywhere in the chain.\n\n**Glass-box** = every step is traceable and auditable, but some components (like the sentence encoder internally) have complexity you can observe the *output* of without needing to inspect the weights.\n\n---\n\n### Layer 1 â€” CompositionRule: fully crystal-box\n\nEvery synthetic edge carries a provenance string:\n```\nrule:compose:directed_by+born_inâ†’directed_by:born_in\n  |path:Inceptionâ†’Christopher_Nolanâ†’London\n```\n\nA human â€” or an auditor â€” can read that string and verify: \"yes, Inception was directed by Christopher Nolan, and Christopher Nolan was born in London, therefore this edge is correct.\" The logical basis is fully exposed and checkable. There is no probability here â€” it is a deduction.\n\n---\n\n### Layer 2 â€” IncompletenessRepairEngine: glass-box\n\nThe bridge edges carry:\n```\nconfidence = cosine_similarity(emb(dead_end), emb(candidate))\n           Ã— relation_prior.score(path_to_dead_end)\n```\n\nYou can inspect the exact score, the exact nodes involved, and the exact path that triggered the repair. The `SentenceEngine` is already a load-bearing component of the CSA formula (`0.4 Ã— cosine_sim`) â€” using it here is the same tool, same role. The only \"opacity\" is inside the transformer model that produced the embeddings, but that model is not trained on CWQ data, not modified by it, and produces the same output for the same input regardless of the graph.\n\n---\n\n### What's preserved\n\nEvery one of CEREBRUM's novel contributions remains central:\n\n| Novel Component | Role in CWQ fix |\n|----------------|-----------------|\n| **DSCF attention heads** | Still partitions the augmented graph into semantic communities |\n| **CSA formula** | Still scores every candidate edge with the same 5-term formula |\n| **BeamTraversal** | Still the reasoning engine â€” now traverses the completed graph |\n| **CompositionRule** | Expanded from named-pair to generalized â€” still provable logic |\n| **IncompletenessRepairEngine** | Already part of the framework (IKGWQ Phase 28) |\n| **RelationPathPrior** | Still available for scoring path relation sequences |\n\n---\n\n### The philosophical line\n\nThe distinguishing criterion is: **does the system learn from the task data?**\n\n- LLM fine-tuning: weights change based on CWQ QA pairs â†’ black-box\n- TransE trained on CWQ triples: weights change based on CWQ graph â†’ trained, dataset-specific\n- **CompositionRule**: reads existing edges, writes logical deductions â†’ no weights, no learning\n- **IncompletenessRepairEngine**: reads embeddings from a fixed general encoder, computes similarity â†’ no weights change, no CWQ-specific learning\n\nCEREBRUM's contract â€” *every answer is a verifiable path through graph edges* â€” is maintained. The synthetic edges added by the repair engine are themselves graph edges with provenance. A path through a synthetic edge reads: \"I traversed Aâ†’B (known), then Bâ†’C (inferred because cosine_sim=0.87), then Câ†’D (known), therefore the answer is D.\" That chain is auditable at every hop.\n\nThe only thing that changes is the graph gets richer before reasoning begins â€” the same way a human researcher might first draw inferences on a whiteboard before answering a question, rather than reasoning purely from raw facts."}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":3,"cache_creation_input_tokens":1775,"cache_read_input_tokens":161413,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":1775},"output_tokens":0,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"}},"requestId":"req_011CZdL6jnXVjTkam6RtS8L9","type":"assistant","uuid":"946c18e5-174f-497b-912a-c2b8c507effd","timestamp":"2026-04-01T16:01:15.767Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"E:\\Development\\Cerebrum","sessionId":"1b39a2ba-e177-466c-ade0-cb0f0629b3b0","version":"2.1.87","gitBranch":"main","slug":"dynamic-marinating-meadow"}

A complete solution for ingesting, enhancing and reading language graphs that is completely transparent and traceable.\" The solution must require **zero training on task-specific data** â€” pre-trained models (SentenceEngine) are acceptable as fixed inference tools, but TransE/GCN/fine-tuning are not.\n\n   Earlier requests in the session that are complete:\n   - Fix CWQ setup script schema (done)\n   - Run WebQSP `--optimized` variant (done: 6.27% H@1, 20.84% H@10)\n   - Fix CWQ eval bugs and run baseline evaluations (done)\n   - Rebuild CWQ scaffold in name-string-only format (done)\n\n2. Key Technical Concepts:\n   - **CEREBRUM**: Community-Structured Graph Attention framework. THALAMUS (ingestion) â†’ Enhancement Layer (NEW) â†’ CORTEX (reasoning)\n   - **DSCF**: Dual/Triple Signal Community Fusion â€” communities act as attention heads\n   - **CSA Formula**: `a(u,v,k) = sigmoid(0.4*cosine_sim + 0.4*community_score + 0.1*edge_type - 0.05*dist + 0.05*hop_decay)`\n   - **CWQ scaffold problem**: mixing MID-format nodes (`/m/xxxxx` from WebQSP freebase) with name-string nodes (from CWQ graph column) creates two disconnected subgraphs, causing DSCF to produce ~900K isolated communities\n   - **RoG-CWQ schema**: `id`, `question`, `answer` (list[str]), `q_entity` (list[str] seeds), `a_entity` (list[str] answers), `graph` (list[list[str]] triples), no `compositionality_type` field\n   - **CompositionRule** (existing): materializes Aâ†’C from Aâ†’R1â†’Bâ†’R2â†’C, but requires explicit `r1`, `r2` parameters\n   - **GeneralizedCompositionRule** (to be built): auto-enumerates all (R1,R2) pairs, materializes shortcuts with `min_occurrences` filter\n   - **GraphBridgeEngine** (to be built): finds frontier nodes (low-degree) in small disconnected components, uses batch cosine similarity on SentenceEngine embeddings to synthesize bridge edges across component boundaries\n   - **Crystal-box vs Glass-box**: CompositionRule = crystal-box (fully deterministic, logical provenance); IncompletenessRepairEngine/GraphBridgeEngine = glass-box (transparent confidence scores, uses pre-trained SentenceEngine as fixed tool)\n   - **IncompletenessRepairEngine** (`core/repair_engine.py`): existing reactive bridge engine (needs traversal paths first); GraphBridgeEngine is the proactive version (runs before traversal)\n   - **CerebrumGraph** (`core/cerebrum.py`): unified pipeline class â€” `from_kb()` â†’ `complete()` â†’ `build()` â†’ `query()`; `enhance()` will slot between `complete()` and `build()`\n\n3. Files and Code Sections:\n   - **`scripts/setup_cwq_data.py`** (completely rewritten twice)\n     - First rewrite: fixed field names from `entities`/`answers` to `q_entity`/`a_entity`/`graph` (RoG schema)\n     - Second rewrite: `_build_scaffold()` changed to use ONLY CWQ graph column triples (name-string format), removing all WebQSP MID triples. No longer calls `_normalise_node()` on graph triples (keeps raw name strings). Key code:\n     ```python\n     def _build_scaffold(ds) -> int:\n         triples: Set[Tuple[str, str, str]] = set()\n         _progress(\"Aggregating KG triples from CWQ graph column (name-string format)...\")\n         for split_name in ds:\n             split = ds[split_name]\n             for example in split:\n                 graph = example.get(\"graph\") or []\n                 for triple in graph:\n                     if isinstance(triple, (list, tuple)) and len(triple) == 3:\n                         s = str(triple[0]).strip()\n                         r = str(triple[1]).strip()\n                         o = str(triple[2]).strip()\n                         if s and r and o:\n                             triples.add((s, r, o))\n     ```\n     - Coverage check fixed: changed `if a.get(\"AnswerType\") == \"Entity\"` to `if a.get(\"AnswerArgument\")`\n     - Result: 7,269,449 triples, 98.1% coverage (seeds 99.0%, answers 99.2%)\n\n   - **`benchmarks/cwq_eval.py`** (multiple fixes)\n     - Bug fix 1 (arg order): `coarsen_communities(raw_cmap, G, ...)` â†’ `coarsen_communities(G, raw_cmap, target_max=args.coarsen_target)` at line 605\n     - Bug fix 2 (max_hop): `max_hop=3` â†’ `max_hop=4` at line 635\n     - Bug fix 3 (attribute name): `getattr(graph_prior, \"_relation_scores\", {})` â†’ `getattr(graph_prior, \"_rel_score\", {})` at line 661\n     - Default coarsen target: changed from 200 to 300 at line 548\n\n   - **`core/graph_completion.py`** (to be modified â€” read but not yet edited)\n     - Existing `CompositionRule` requires explicit `r1`, `r2`, `composed_relation` parameters\n     - `GeneralizedCompositionRule` needs to be added that auto-enumerates all (R1,R2) pairs\n     - No statistical predictions are made â€” only logical deductions.\n7\t\n8\tAvailable rules\n9\t---------------\n10\tInverseRule\n11\t    Add B -R_inv-> A whenever A -R-> B exists, for a known symmetric or\n12\t    inverse-relation pair.  Backed by the source edge.\n13\t\n14\tCompositionRule\n15\t    Add A -R_composed-> C whenever A -R1-> B -R2-> C exists, for a known\n16\t    composable relation pair.  Backed by the two-hop path (A,B,C).\n17\t\n18\tAll synthetic edges carry:\n19\t  relation    : the logical relation name\n20\t  synthetic   : True\n21\t  confidence  : min(conf(backing edges)) \"rule:compose:{R1}+{R2}â†’{Rc}|path:{a}â†’{b}â†’{c}\"\n32\t\"\"\"\n33\tfrom __future__ import annotations\n34\t\n35\timport logging\n36\tfrom typing import Dict, List, Optional, Set, ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n46\t# Base class\n47\t# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n48\t\n49\tclass CompletionRule:\n50\t    \"\"\"Abstract base for provable graph completion rules.\"\"\"\n51\t\n52\t    def apply(self, adapter: NetworkXAdapter) -> int:\n53\t        \"\"\"\n54\t        Apply this rule to the adapter's underlying graph.\n55\t\n56\t        Returns the number of new edges added.\n57\t        \"\"\"\n58\t        raise NotImplementedError\n59\t\n60\t    def describe(self) -> str:\n61\t        \"\"\"Human-readable description of what this rule does.\"\"\"\n62\t        raise NotImplementedError\n63\t\n64\t\n65\t# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n66\t# InverseRule\n67\t# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n68\t\n69\tclass InverseRule(CompletionRule):\n70\t    \"\"\"\n71\t    Materialize the inverse (or symmetric twin) of a known relation.\n72\t\n73\t    For symmetric relations (e.g. MARRIED_TO):\n74\t        InverseRule(\"MARRIED_TO\")\n75\t        â†’ adds B -MARRIED_TO-> A for every A -MARRIED_TO-> B\n76\t\n77\t    For asymmetric inverse pairs (e.g. directed_by / director_of):\n78\t        InverseRule(\"directed_by\", \"director_of\")\n79\t        â†’ adds B -director_of-> A for every A -directed_by-> B\n80\t\n81\t    Logical basis\n82\t    -------------\n83\t    The rule encodes a domain fact about the relation's semantics â€” not a\n84\t    statistical guess.  The caller is asserting that the relation is known\n85\t    to be symmetric (or to have a named inverse), which is a statement about\n86\t    the ontology, not about the data.\n87\t\n88\t    Provenance\n89\t    ----------\n90\t    Every synthetic edge records:\n91\t        provenance = \"rule:inverse:{relation}â†’{inverse}|source:{src}â†’{tgt}\"\n92\t        confidence = source edge confidence\n93\t        synthetic  = True\n94\t    \"\"\"\n95\t\n96\t    def __init__(self, relation: str, inverse_relation: Optional[str] = None):\n97\t        \"\"\"\n98\t        Parameters\n99\t        ----------\n100\t        relation         : the forward relation to match\n101\t        inverse_relation : the relation name for the reverse edge.\n102\t                           Defaults to ``relation`` (symmetric case).\n103\t        \"\"\"\n104\t        self.relation         = relation\n105\t        self.inverse_relation = inverse_relation or relation\n106\t\n107\t    def describe(self) -> str:\n108\t        if self.inverse_relation == self.relation:\n109\t            return f\"InverseRule: {self.relation!r} is symmetric â†’ add reverse edges\"\n110\t        return (\n111\t            f\"InverseRule: {self.relation!r} â†’ \"\n112\t            f\"inverse is {self.inverse_relation!r}\"\n113\t        )\n114\t\n115\t    def apply(self, adapter: NetworkXAdapter) -> int:\n116\t        G   = adapter._G\n117\t        new = 0\n118\t\n119\t        # Collect edges to add (don't mutate while iterating)\n120\t        to_add: List[Tuple] = []\n121\t        for src, tgt, data in list(G.edges(data=True)):\n122\t            if data.get(\"relation\") != self.relation:\n123\t           
     continue\n124\t            # Skip if the inverse already exists\n125\t            if G.has_edge(tgt, src):\n126\t                existing = G.get_edge_data(tgt, src) or {}\n127\t                if existing.get(\"relation\") == self.inverse_relation:\n128\t                    continue\n129\t\n130\t            provenance = (\n131\t                f\"rule:inverse:{self.relation}â†’{self.inverse_relation}\"\n132\t                f\"|source:{src}â†’{tgt}\"\n133\t            )\n134\t            to_add.append((\n135\t                tgt,\n136\t            
    src,\n137\t                {\n138\t                    \"relation\":   self.inverse_relation,\n139\t                    \"weight\":     float(data.get(\"weight\", 1.0)),\n140\t                    \"confidence\": float(data.get(\"confidence\", 1.0)),\n141\t                    \"provenance\": provenance,\n142\t                    \"synthetic\":  True,\n143\t                    \"valid_from\": data.get(\"valid_from\"),\n144\t                  
  \"valid_to\":   data.get(\"valid_to\"),\n145\t                },\n146\t            ))\n147\t\n148\t        for tgt, src, attrs in to_add:\n149\t            G.add_edge(tgt, src, **attrs)\n150\t            new += 1\n151\t\n152\t        if new:\n153\t            logger.debug(\n154\t                \"InverseRule(%r â†’ %r): added %d edges\",\n155\t                self.relation, self.inverse_relation, new,\n156\t            )\n157\t            # Invalidate n-gram index if present\n158\t            if hasattr(adapter, \"_ngram_index\"):\n159\t               
 del adapter._ngram_index\n160\t\n161\t        return new\n162\t\n163\t\n164\t# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n165\t# CompositionRule\n166\t# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n167\t\n168\tclass CompositionRule(CompletionRule):\n169\t    \"\"\"\n170\t    Materialize a composed relation A -Rc-> C whenever A -R1-> B -R2-> C exists.\n171\t\n172\t    Example: if R1=\"directed_by\" and R2=\"born_in\", then composing them\n173\t    produces A -directed_by:born_in-> C meaning \"the director of A was born\n174\t    in C\".  Give it a meaningful name via ``composed_relation``.\n175\t\n176\t    Logical basis\n177\t    -------------\n178\t    The synthetic edge is not a guess â€” it is a shortcut backed by the\n179\t    two-hop path (A, B, C) that already exists in the graph.  The path is\n180\t    cited in the provenance of every added edge.  If the backing path is\n181\t    ever removed, the synthetic edge loses its citation but can be recomputed.\n182\t\n183\t    This is identical to what a query engine would compute on-the-fly for a\n184\t    two-hop SPARQL join; materializing it makes it traversable in one hop.\n185\t\n186\t    Parameters\n187\t    ----------\n188\t    r1                : first relation in the chain\n189\t    r2              
  : second relation in the chain\n190\t    composed_relation : name for the materialized shortcut edge\n191\t    min_occurrences   : only materialize if this (A, C) pair is reached by\n192\t                        at least ``min_occurrences`` distinct intermediate B\n193\t                        nodes.  Default 1 (add on any single evidence path).\n194\t                        Raise to 2+ to require corroborating evidence.\n195\t    max_edges         : safety cap on total edges added (default 500_000)\n196\t\n197\t    Provenance\n198\t    ----------\n199\t    Every synthetic edge records:\n200\t        provenance = \"rule:compose:{R1}+{R2}â†’{Rc}|path:{a}â†’{b}â†’{c}\"\n201\t                     (first supporting path only; confidence = weakest-link)\n202\t        synthetic  = True\n203\t    \"\"\"\n204\t\n205\t    def __init__(\n206\t        self,\n207\t        r1:                str,\n208\t        r2:                str,\n209\t        composed_relation: str,\n210\t        min_occurrences:   int = 1,\n211\t        max_edges:         int = 500_000,\n212\t    ):\n213\t        self.r1                = r1\n214\t        self.r2                = r2\n215\t        self.composed_relation = composed_relation\n216\t        self.min_occurrences   = min_occurrences\n217\t        self.max_edges         = max_edges\n218\t\n219\t    def describe(self) -> str:\n220\t        return (\n221\t            f\"CompositionRule: {self.r1!r} + {self.r2!r} â†’ \"\n222\t            f\"{self.composed_relation!r}  \"\n223\t            f\"(min_occurrences={self.min_occurrences})\"\n224\t        )\n225\t\n226\t    def apply(self, adapter: NetworkXAdapter) -> int:\n227\t        G   = adapter._G\n228\t        new = 0\n229\t\n230\t        # Index: A â†’ {B nodes reachable via R1}\n231\t        # Then for each B, follow R2 to find C nodes.\n232\t        # Group by (A, C) to count distinct intermediates.\n233\t\n234\t        # intermediate_data[(a, c)] = list of (b, conf_ab, conf_bc, prov_path)\n235\t        from collections import defaultdict\n236\t        evidence: Dict[\n237\t            Tuple[str, str],\n238\t            List[Tuple[str, float, float, str]]\n239\t        ] = defaultdict(list)\n240\t\n241\t        for a, b, d_ab in G.edges(data=True):\n242\t            if d_ab.get(\"relation\") != self.r1:\n243\t                continue\n244\t            conf_ab = float(d_ab.get(\"confidence\", 1.0))\n245\t\n246\t            for _b, c, d_bc in G.out_edges(b, data=True) if G.is_directed() \\\n247\t                    else G.edges(b, data=True):\n248\t               
 if _b != b:\n249\t                    continue\n250\t                if d_bc.get(\"relation\") != self.r2:\n251\t                    continue\n252\t                if c == a:          # skip trivial cycles\n253\t           
         continue\n254\t                conf_bc = float(d_bc.get(\"confidence\", 1.0))\n255\t                prov    = (\n256\t                    f\"rule:compose:{self.r1}+{self.r2}â†’{self.composed_relation}\"\n257\t                    f\"|path:{a}â†’{b}â†’{c}\"\n258\t                )\n259\t                evidence[(a, c)].append((b, conf_ab, conf_bc, prov))\n260\t\n261\t        to_add: List[Tuple] = []\n262\t        for (a, c), paths in evidence.items():\n263\t            if len(paths) < self.min_occurrences:\n264\t                continue\n265\t            if G.has_edge(a, c):\n266\t                existing = G.get_edge_data(a, c) or {}\n267\t                
if existing.get(\"relation\") == self.composed_relation:\n268\t                    continue\n269\t\n270\t            # Use the highest-confidence path as the primary citation\n271\t            best = max(paths, key=lambda x: min(x[1], x[2]))\n272\t            b_best, conf_ab, conf_bc, prov = best\n273\t            confidence = min(conf_ab, conf_bc)\n274\t\n275\t            to_add.append((\n276\t                a,\n277\t                c,\n278\t                {\n279\t                    \"relation\":   self.composed_relation,\n280\t                    \"weight\":     confidence,\n281\t                    \"confidence\": confidence,\n282\t                    \"provenance\": prov,\n283\t                    \"synthetic\":  True,\n284\t                },\n285\t            ))\n286\t\n287\t            if len(to_add) >= self.max_edges:\n288\t                logger.warning(\n289\t           
         \"CompositionRule(%r+%r): hit max_edges cap %d\",\n290\t                    self.r1, self.r2, self.max_edges,\n291\t                )\n292\t                break\n293\t\n294\t        for a, c, attrs in to_add:\n295\t            G.add_edge(a, c, **attrs)\n296\t            new += 1\n297\t\n298\t        if new:\n299\t           
 logger.debug(\n300\t                \"CompositionRule(%r+%r â†’ %r): added %d edges\",\n301\t                self.r1, self.r2, self.composed_relation, new,\n302\t            )\n303\t            if hasattr(adapter, \"_ngram_index\"):\n304\t                del adapter._ngram_index\n305\t\n306\t        return new\n307\t\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. No statistical predictions are made â€” only logical deductions.\n\nAvailable rules\n---------------\nInverseRule\n    Add B -R_inv-> A whenever A -R-> B exists, for a known symmetric or\n    inverse-relation pair.  Backed by the source edge.\n\nCompositionRule\n    Add A -R_composed-> C whenever A -R1-> B -R2-> C exists, for a known\n    composable relation pair.  Backed by the two-hop path (A,B,C).\n\nAll synthetic edges carry:\n  relation    : the logical relation name\n  synthetic   : True\n  confidence  : min(conf(backing edges))   [weakest-link rule]\n  provenance  : structured string citing the rule and backing evidence\n  weight      : min(weight(backing edges))\n\nProvenenance format\n-------------------\n  InverseRule:\n    \"rule:inverse:{R}â†’{R_inv}|source:{src_id}â†’{tgt_id}\"\n\n  CompositionRule:\n    \"rule:compose:{R1}+{R2}â†’{Rc}|path:{a}â†’{b}â†’{c}\"\n\"\"\"\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport logging\nfrom typing import Dict, List, Optional, Set, Tuple\n\nimport networkx as nx\n\nfrom adapters.networkx_adapter import NetworkXAdapter\n\nlogger = logging.getLogger(\"cerebrum.graph_completion\")\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Base class\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nclass CompletionRule:\n    \"\"\"Abstract base for provable graph completion rules.\"\"\"\n\n    def apply(self, adapter: NetworkXAdapter) -> int:\n        \"\"\"\n        Apply this rule to the adapter's underlying graph.\n\n        Returns the number of new edges added.\n        \"\"\"\n        raise NotImplementedError\n\n    def describe(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Human-readable description of what this rule does.\"\"\"\n        raise NotImplementedError\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# InverseRule\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nclass InverseRule(CompletionRule):\n    \"\"\"\n    Materialize the inverse (or symmetric twin) of a known relation.\n\n    For symmetric relations (e.g. MARRIED_TO):\n        InverseRule(\"MARRIED_TO\")\n        â†’ adds B -MARRIED_TO-> A for every A -MARRIED_TO-> B\n\n    For asymmetric inverse pairs (e.g. directed_by / director_of):\n        InverseRule(\"directed_by\", \"director_of\")\n        â†’ adds B -director_of-> A for every A -directed_by-> B\n\n    Logical basis\n    -------------\n    The rule encodes a domain fact about the relation's semantics â€” not a\n    statistical guess.  The caller is asserting that the relation is known\n    to be symmetric (or to have a named inverse), which is a statement about\n    the ontology, not about the data.\n\n    Provenance\n    ----------\n    Every synthetic edge records:\n        provenance = \"rule:inverse:{relation}â†’{inverse}|source:{src}â†’{tgt}\"\n        confidence = source edge confidence\n        synthetic  = True\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, relation: str, inverse_relation: Optional[str] = None):\n        \"\"\"\n        Parameters\n        ----------\n        relation         : the forward relation to match\n        inverse_relation : the relation name for the reverse edge.\n                           Defaults to ``relation`` (symmetric case).\n        \"\"\"\n        self.relation         = relation\n        self.inverse_relation = inverse_relation or relation\n\n    def describe(self) -> str:\n        if self.inverse_relation == self.relation:\n            return f\"InverseRule: {self.relation!r} is symmetric â†’ add reverse edges\"\n        return (\n            f\"InverseRule: {self.relation!r} â†’ \"\n            f\"inverse is {self.inverse_relation!r}\"\n        )\n\n    def apply(self, adapter: NetworkXAdapter) -> int:\n        G   = adapter._G\n        new = 0\n\n        # Collect edges to add (don't mutate while iterating)\n        to_add: List[Tuple] = []\n        for src, tgt, data in list(G.edges(data=True)):\n            if data.get(\"relation\") != self.relation:\n             
   continue\n            # Skip if the inverse already exists\n            if G.has_edge(tgt, src):\n           
     existing = G.get_edge_data(tgt, src) or {}\n                if existing.get(\"relation\") == self.inverse_relation:\n                    continue\n\n            provenance = (\n                f\"rule:inverse:{self.relation}â†’{self.inverse_relation}\"\n                f\"|source:{src}â†’{tgt}\"\n            )\n            to_add.append((\n                tgt,\n                src,\n                {\n                    \"relation\":   self.inverse_relation,\n                    \"weight\":     float(data.get(\"weight\", 1.0)),\n                   
 \"confidence\": float(data.get(\"confidence\", 1.0)),\n                    \"provenance\": provenance,\n                    \"synthetic\":  True,\n                    \"valid_from\": data.get(\"valid_from\"),\n             
       \"valid_to\":   data.get(\"valid_to\"),\n                },\n            ))\n\n        for tgt, src, attrs in to_add:\n            G.add_edge(tgt, src, **attrs)\n            new += 1\n\n        if new:\n            logger.debug(\n                \"InverseRule(%r â†’ %r): added %d edges\",\n                self.relation, self.inverse_relation, new,\n            )\n            # Invalidate n-gram index if present\n            if hasattr(adapter, \"_ngram_index\"):\n                del adapter._ngram_index\n\n        return new\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# CompositionRule\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nclass CompositionRule(CompletionRule):\n    \"\"\"\n    Materialize a composed relation A -Rc-> C whenever A -R1-> B -R2-> C exists.\n\n    Example: if R1=\"directed_by\" and R2=\"born_in\", then composing them\n    produces A -directed_by:born_in-> C meaning \"the director of A was born\n    in C\".  Give it a meaningful name via ``composed_relation``.\n\n    Logical basis\n    -------------\n    The synthetic edge is not a guess â€” it is a shortcut backed by the\n    two-hop path (A, B, C) that already exists in the graph.  The path is\n    cited in the provenance of every added edge.  If the backing path is\n    ever removed, the synthetic edge loses its citation but can be recomputed.\n\n    This is identical to what a query engine would compute on-the-fly for a\n    two-hop SPARQL join; materializing it makes it traversable in one hop.\n\n    Parameters\n    ----------\n    r1                : first relation in the chain\n    r2          
      : second relation in the chain\n    composed_relation : name for the materialized shortcut edge\n    min_occurrences   : only materialize if this (A, C) pair is reached by\n                        at least ``min_occurrences`` distinct intermediate B\n                        nodes.  Default 1 (add on any single evidence path).\n                        Raise to 2+ to require corroborating evidence.\n    max_edges         : safety cap on total edges added (default 500_000)\n\n    Provenance\n    ----------\n    Every synthetic edge records:\n        provenance = \"rule:compose:{R1}+{R2}â†’{Rc}|path:{a}â†’{b}â†’{c}\"\n                     (first supporting path only; confidence = weakest-link)\n        synthetic  = True\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        r1:                str,\n        r2:                str,\n        composed_relation: str,\n        min_occurrences:   int = 1,\n        max_edges:         int = 500_000,\n    ):\n        self.r1                = r1\n        self.r2                = r2\n        self.composed_relation = composed_relation\n        self.min_occurrences   = min_occurrences\n        self.max_edges         = max_edges\n\n    def describe(self) -> str:\n        return (\n            f\"CompositionRule: {self.r1!r} + {self.r2!r} â†’ \"\n            f\"{self.composed_relation!r}  \"\n            f\"(min_occurrences={self.min_occurrences})\"\n        )\n\n    def apply(self, adapter: NetworkXAdapter) -> int:\n        G   = adapter._G\n        new = 0\n\n        # Index: A â†’ {B nodes reachable via R1}\n        # Then for each B, follow R2 to find C nodes.\n        # Group by (A, C) to count distinct intermediates.\n\n        # intermediate_data[(a, c)] = list of (b, conf_ab, conf_bc, prov_path)\n        from collections import defaultdict\n        evidence: Dict[\n            Tuple[str, str],\n            List[Tuple[str, float, float, str]]\n        ] = defaultdict(list)\n\n        for a, b, d_ab in G.edges(data=True):\n            if d_ab.get(\"relation\") != self.r1:\n                continue\n            conf_ab = float(d_ab.get(\"confidence\", 1.0))\n\n            for _b, c, d_bc in G.out_edges(b, data=True) if G.is_directed() \\\n                   
 else G.edges(b, data=True):\n                if _b != b:\n                    continue\n                if d_bc.get(\"relation\") != self.r2:\n                    continue\n                if c == a:          # skip trivial cycles\n                    continue\n                conf_bc = float(d_bc.get(\"confidence\", 1.0))\n         
       prov    = (\n                    f\"rule:compose:{self.r1}+{self.r2}â†’{self.composed_relation}\"\n                    f\"|path:{a}â†’{b}â†’{c}\"\n                )\n                evidence[(a, c)].append((b, conf_ab, conf_bc, prov))\n\n        to_add: List[Tuple] = []\n        for (a, c), paths in evidence.items():\n         
   if len(paths) < self.min_occurrences:\n                continue\n            if G.has_edge(a, c):\n          
      existing = G.get_edge_data(a, c) or {}\n                if existing.get(\"relation\") == self.composed_relation:\n                    continue\n\n            # Use the highest-confidence path as the primary citation\n            best = max(paths, key=lambda x: min(x[1], x[2]))\n            b_best, conf_ab, conf_bc, prov = best\n            confidence = min(conf_ab, conf_bc)\n\n            to_add.append((\n                a,\n             
   c,\n                {\n                    \"relation\":   self.composed_relation,\n                    \"weight\":     confidence,\n                    \"confidence\": confidence,\n                    \"provenance\": prov,\n                    \"synthetic\":  True,\n                },\n            ))\n\n            if len(to_add) >= self.max_edges:\n                logger.warning(\n                    \"CompositionRule(%r+%r): hit max_edges cap %d\",\n                    self.r1, self.r2, self.max_edges,\n                )\n                break\n\n        for a, c, attrs in to_add:\n            G.add_edge(a, c, **attrs)\n            new += 1\n\n        if new:\n            logger.debug(\n                \"CompositionRule(%r+%r â†’ %r): added %d edges\",\n              
  self.r1, self.r2, self.composed_relation, new,\n            )\n            if hasattr(adapter, \"_ngram_index\"):\n                del adapter._ngram_index\n\n        return new\n","numLines":307,"startLine":1,"totalLines":307}},"sourceToolAssistantUUID":"2b0839d4-b68d-4fee-96ec-bae3f993318b","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"E:\\Development\\Cerebrum","sessionId":"1b39a2ba-e177-466c-ade0-cb0f0629b3b0","version":"2.1.87","gitBranch":"main","slug":"dynamic-marinating-meadow"}
{"parentUuid":"c8c9c19d-1652-4462-b388-ec3f7af008f2","isSidechain":false,"promptId":"6439507d-d1c3-4c31-b699-ae59e2e5bc28","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_014w7SqcPmt4hoeNJAhz6NDk","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t\"\"\"\n2\tCerebrumGraph â€” unified THALAMUS â†’ CORTEX pipeline.\n3\t\n4\tThis is the main entry point for building and querying a knowledge graph\n5\twith CEREBRUM.  It replaces the manual wiring that previously lived inside\n6\tindividual benchmark scripts.\n7\t\n8\tPipeline\n9\t--------\n10\tTHALAMUS (ingestion):\n11\t  1. Load graph via adapter (from_kb / from_csv / from_triples / from_adapter)\n12\t  2. Optional: apply provable completion rules (InverseRule, CompositionRule)\n13\t  3. Encode entity embeddings (RandomEngine or SentenceEngine)\n14\t  4. Detect communities (DSCF) and optionally coarsen\n15\t  5. Build CSA attention engine with community distance matrix\n16\t\n17\tCORTEX (reasoning):\n18\t  6. BeamTraversal over the CSA-weighted graph\n19\t  7. AnswerExtractor with path scoring and convergence voting\n20\t\n21\tUsage\n22\t-----\n23\t    from core.cerebrum import CerebrumGraph\n24\t    from core.graph_completion import InverseRule, CompositionRule\n25\t\n26\t    # Build from a pipe-separated triples file (MetaQA KB format)\n27\t    graph = CerebrumGraph.from_kb(\n28\t        \"benchmarks/data/metaqa/kb.txt\",\n29\t        sep=\"|\",\n30\t        embeddings=\"sentence\",\n31\t    )\n32\t\n33\t    # Optional: augment with provable inference rules before building\n34\t    graph.complete([\n35\t        InverseRule(\"starred_actors\"),          # symmetric\n36\t        InverseRule(\"directed_by\", \"director_of\"),\n37\t    ])\n38\t\n39\t    # Run THALAMUS pipeline (embeddings + communities + CSA)\n40\t    graph.build(cache_dir=\"cache/metaqa\", min_community_size=20)\n41\t\n42\t    # Query\n43\t    answers = graph.query([\"Tom Hanks\"], top_k=10, max_hop=2, min_hop=1)\n44\t    for a in answers:\n45\t        print(a.entity_id, a.score, a.path_confidence)\n46\t\"\"\"\n47\tfrom __future__ import annotations\n48\t\n49\timport logging\n50\timport pickle\n51\tfrom pathlib import Path\n52\tfrom typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional, Union\n53\t\n54\timport networkx as nx\n55\timport numpy as np\n56\t\n57\tfrom adapters.networkx_adapter import NetworkXAdapter\n58\tfrom core.attention_engine import CSAEngine\n59\tfrom core.embedding_engine import EmbeddingEngine, RandomEngine\n60\tfrom core.graph_adapter import GraphAdapter\n61\tfrom reasoning.answer_extractor import Answer, extract\n62\tfrom reasoning.traversal import BeamTraversal\n63\t\n64\tlogger = logging.getLogger(\"cerebrum.graph\")\n65\t\n66\t\n67\t# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n68\t# CerebrumGraph\n69\t# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n70\t\n71\tclass CerebrumGraph:\n72\t    \"\"\"\n73\t    Encapsulates the full THALAMUS â†’ CORTEX pipeline for a single KG.\n74\t\n75\t    Parameters\n76\t    ----------\n77\t    adapter          : NetworkXAdapter (or any GraphAdapter) wrapping the graph\n78\t    embedding_engine : EmbeddingEngine instance.  Defaults to RandomEngine(64).\n79\t    beam_width       : default beam width for traversal (default 10)\n80\t    max_hop          : maximum hop depth for traversal (default 3)\n81\t    max_neighbors    : per-node neighbor expansion cap (default 100)\n82\t    probabilistic    : enable Bayesian Beta-distribution path model (default False)\n83\t    warm_start_strength : warm-start strength for probabilistic mode (default 0)\n84\t    \"\"\"\n85\t\n86\t    def __init__(\n87\t        self,\n88\t        adapter:              GraphAdapter,\n89\t        embedding_engine:     Optional[EmbeddingEngine] = None,\n90\t        beam_width:           int   = 10,\n91\t        max_hop:              int   = 3,\n92\t        max_neighbors:        int   = 100,\n93\t        probabilistic:        bool  = False,\n94\t        warm_start_strength:  float = 0.0,\n95\t    ):\n96\t        self.adapter             = adapter\n97\t        self._embedding_engine   = embedding_engine or RandomEngine(dim=64)\n98\t        self._beam_width         = beam_width\n99\t        self._max_hop            = max_hop\n100\t        self._max_neighbors      = max_neighbors\n101\t        self._probabilistic      = probabilistic\n102\t        self._warm_start_strength = warm_start_strength\n103\t\n104\t        self._csa:       Optional[CSAEngine]    = None\n105\t        self._traversal: Optional[BeamTraversal] = None\n106\t        self._built      = False\n107\t\n108\t    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n109\t    # Factory methods\n110\t    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n111\t\n112\t    @classmethod\n113\t    def from_kb(\n114\t        cls,\n115\t        path:          
   Union[str, Path],\n116\t        sep:              str  = \"|\",\n117\t        directed:         bool = True,\n118\t        embeddings:       str  = \"random\",\n119\t        embedding_dim:    int  = 64,\n120\t        pipeline=None,\n121\t        **kwargs,\n122\t    ) -> \"CerebrumGraph\":\n123\t        \"\"\"\n124\t        Load from a flat triples file (one triple per line).\n125\t\n126\t        Format:  head<sep>relation<sep>tail\n127\t\n128\t        Parameters\n129\t        ----------\n130\t        path          : path to the KB file\n131\t        sep           : field separator (default \"|\" for MetaQA)\n132\t        directed      : use DiGraph if True (default), Graph if False\n133\t        embeddings    : \"random\" or \"sentence\"\n134\t        embedding_dim : dimension for random embeddings (ignored for sentence)\n135\t        pipeline      : optional IngestionPipeline for normalisation\n136\t        \"\"\"\n137\t        G = nx.DiGraph() if directed else nx.Graph()\n138\t        with open(path, encoding=\"utf-8\") as f:\n139\t            for line in f:\n140\t                line = line.strip()\n141\t                if not line or line.startswith(\"#\"):\n142\t                    continue\n143\t                parts = line.split(sep)\n144\t                if len(parts) < 3:\n145\t                    continue\n146\t                s = parts[0].strip()\n147\t                r = parts[1].strip()\n148\t                o = parts[2].strip()\n149\t                if not s or not r or not o:\n150\t                    continue\n151\t\n152\t                if pipeline is not None:\n153\t                    edge = pipeline.process(s, o, r)\n154\t                    G.add_edge(\n155\t                        edge.source, edge.target,\n156\t                        relation=edge.relation,\n157\t                        confidence=float(edge.confidence),\n158\t                  
      provenance=str(edge.provenance),\n159\t                        weight=float(edge.weight),\n160\t          
          )\n161\t                else:\n162\t                    G.add_edge(s, o, relation=r, weight=1.0, confidence=1.0,\n163\t                               provenance=\"kb_file\")\n164\t\n165\t        adapter = NetworkXAdapter(G)\n166\t        eng     = cls._make_engine(embeddings, embedding_dim)\n167\t        return cls(adapter, embedding_engine=eng, **kwargs)\n168\t\n169\t    @classmethod\n170\t    def from_csv(\n171\t        cls,\n172\t        path:          Union[str, Path],\n173\t        embeddings:    str = \"random\",\n174\t        embedding_dim: int = 64,\n175\t        **kwargs,\n176\t    ) -> \"CerebrumGraph\":\n177\t        \"\"\"\n178\t        Load from a CSV triples file (CEREBRUM CSV adapter format).\n179\t        \"\"\"\n180\t        from adapters.csv_adapter import load_csv_adapter\n181\t        adapter = load_csv_adapter(str(path))\n182\t        eng     = cls._make_engine(embeddings, embedding_dim)\n183\t        return cls(adapter, embedding_engine=eng, **kwargs)\n184\t\n185\t    @classmethod\n186\t    def from_triples(\n187\t        cls,\n188\t        triples:       List[tuple],\n189\t        directed:      bool = True,\n190\t        pipeline=None,\n191\t        embeddings:    str  = \"random\",\n192\t        embedding_dim: int  = 64,\n193\t        **kwargs,\n194\t    ) -> \"CerebrumGraph\":\n195\t        \"\"\"\n196\t        Build from a list of (subject, predicate, object[, meta_dict]) tuples.\n197\t        \"\"\"\n198\t        adapter = NetworkXAdapter.from_triples(triples, directed=directed,\n199\t                    
                           pipeline=pipeline)\n200\t        eng     = cls._make_engine(embeddings, embedding_dim)\n201\t        return cls(adapter, embedding_engine=eng, **kwargs)\n202\t\n203\t    @classmethod\n204\t    def from_adapter(\n205\t        cls,\n206\t        adapter:       GraphAdapter,\n207\t        embeddings:    str = \"random\",\n208\t        embedding_dim: int = 64,\n209\t        **kwargs,\n210\t    ) -> \"CerebrumGraph\":\n211\t        \"\"\"Wrap an existing adapter (e.g. Neo4jAdapter, FederatedAdapter).\"\"\"\n212\t        eng = cls._make_engine(embeddings, embedding_dim)\n213\t        return cls(adapter, embedding_engine=eng, **kwargs)\n214\t\n215\t    @staticmethod\n216\t    def _make_engine(embeddings: str, dim: int) -> EmbeddingEngine:\n217\t        if embeddings == \"sentence\":\n218\t            from core.embedding_engine import SentenceEngine\n219\t         
   return SentenceEngine()\n220\t        elif embeddings == \"random\":\n221\t            return RandomEngine(dim=dim)\n222\t        else:\n223\t            raise ValueError(\n224\t                f\"Unknown embeddings type {embeddings!r}. \"\n225\t                \"Use 'random' or 'sentence'.\"\n226\t            )\n227\t\n228\t    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n229\t    # Provable graph completion (pre-build step)\n230\t    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n231\t\n232\t    def complete(self, rules: List[Any]) -> \"CerebrumGraph\":\n233\t        \"\"\"\n234\t        Apply provable inference rules to augment the graph before building.\n235\t\n236\t        Every rule adds synthetic edges with full provenance metadata.\n237\t        No statistical predictions are made â€” only logical deductions.\n238\t\n239\t        Parameters\n240\t        ----------\n241\t        rules : list of CompletionRule instances\n242\t          
      (InverseRule, CompositionRule, ...)\n243\t\n244\t        Returns self for chaining.\n245\t        \"\"\"\n246\t        total = 0\n247\t        for rule in rules:\n248\t            n = rule.apply(self.adapter)\n249\t            logger.info(\"%s: +%d edges\", rule.describe(), n)\n250\t            total += n\n251\t        if total:\n252\t            logger.info(\"graph_completion: %d synthetic edges added total\", total)\n253\t        # Invalidate built state â€” must rebuild after completing\n254\t        self._built = False\n255\t        return self\n256\t\n257\t    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n258\t    # THALAMUS build pipeline\n259\t    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n260\t\n261\t    def build(\n262\t        self,\n263\t        cache_dir:            Optional[Union[str, Path]] = None,\n264\t        min_community_size:   int   = 0,\n265\t        coarsen_target:       Optional[int] = None,\n266\t        n_trials:             int   = 1,\n267\t        seed:                 int   = 42,\n268\t        force_rebuild:        bool  = False,\n269\t    ) -> \"CerebrumGraph\":\n270\t        \"\"\"\n271\t        Run the THALAMUS pipeline: embeddings â†’ DSCF communities â†’ CSA.\n272\t\n273\t        Parameters\n274\t        ----------\n275\t        cache_dir           : directory for caching embeddings and communities.\n276\t                              Pass None to skip caching.\n277\t        min_community_size  : merge communities smaller than this (0 = disabled).\n278\t                              Recommended: 20 for MetaQA.\n279\t        coarsen_target      : alternatively, coarsen to at most this many\n280\t                              communities (e.g. 300 for GrailQA).\n281\t        n_trials            : DSCF runs to take the best of (default 1).\n282\t        seed                : random seed for reproducibility.\n283\t        force_rebuild       : ignore cache and recompute everything.\n284\t\n285\t        Returns self for chaining.\n286\t        \"\"\"\n287\t        from core.community_engine import dscf_communities\n288\t        from core.structural_encoder import (\n289\t            build_community_distance_matrix,\n290\t            build_community_graph,\n291\t            adjacent_community_pairs,\n292\t            coarsen_communities,\n293\t        )\n294\t\n295\t        cache = Path(cache_dir) if cache_dir else None\n296\t        if cache:\n297\t            cache.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n298\t\n299\t        G     = self.adapter.to_networkx()\n300\t        nodes = list(G.nodes())\n301\t\n302\t        if not nodes:\n303\t            raise ValueError(\"Graph has no nodes â€” nothing to build.\")\n304\t\n305\t        # ----------------------------------------------------------\n306\t        # 1. Embeddings\n307\t        # ----------------------------------------------------------\n308\t        emb_cache = cache / \"embeddings.pkl\" if cache else None\n309\t\n310\t        if not force_rebuild and emb_cache and emb_cache.exists():\n311\t            logger.info(\"Loading cached embeddings from %s\", emb_cache)\n312\t            with open(emb_cache, \"rb\") as f:\n313\t                self.adapter.embeddings = pickle.load(f)\n314\t        else:\n315\t            # Build label map: node_id â†’ human-readable label\n316\t            entity_labels: Dict[str, str] = {}\n317\t            for n in nodes:\n318\t              
  data  = G.nodes[n]\n319\t                label = data.get(\"label\") or data.get(\"friendly_name\") or str(n)\n320\t                entity_labels[str(n)] = label\n321\t\n322\t            logger.info(\n323\t                
\"Encoding %d entities with %s\",\n324\t                len(entity_labels),\n325\t                type(self._embedding_engine).__name__,\n326\t            )\n327\t            self.adapter.embeddings = self._embedding_engine.encode_entities(\n328\t                entity_labels\n329\t            )\n330\t\n331\t            if emb_cache:\n332\t                with open(emb_cache, \"wb\") as f:\n333\t                    pickle.dump(self.adapter.embeddings, f)\n334\t\n335\t        # ----------------------------------------------------------\n336\t        # 2. Community detection (DSCF)\n337\t        # ----------------------------------------------------------\n338\t        comm_cache = cache / \"communities.pkl\" if cache else None\n339\t        G_und      = G.to_undirected() if G.is_directed() else G\n340\t\n341\t        if not force_rebuild and comm_cache and comm_cache.exists():\n342\t            logger.info(\"Loading cached communities from %s\", comm_cache)\n343\t            with open(comm_cache, \"rb\") as f:\n344\t                parts = pickle.load(f)\n345\t        else:\n346\t            logger.info(\n347\t                \"Running DSCF on %d nodes, %d edges (n_trials=%d)...\",\n348\t                G.number_of_nodes(), G.number_of_edges(), n_trials,\n349\t            )\n350\t            if n_trials > 1:\n351\t         
       from core.community_engine import best_of_n_dscf\n352\t                parts = best_of_n_dscf(G_und, n_trials=n_trials, seed=seed)\n353\t            else:\n354\t                parts = dscf_communities(G_und)\n355\t\n356\t            if comm_cache:\n357\t                with open(comm_cache, \"wb\") as f:\n358\t                
    pickle.dump(parts, f)\n359\t\n360\t        self.adapter._partition = parts\n361\t        cm: Dict = {n: cid for cid, members in enumerate(parts) for n in members}\n362\t        n_raw = len(set(cm.values()))\n363\t        logger.info(\"%d raw DSCF communities\", n_raw)\n364\t\n365\t        # ----------------------------------------------------------\n366\t        # 3. Optional coarsening\n367\t        # ----------------------------------------------------------\n368\t        if min_community_size > 0:\n369\t            from core.structural_encoder import coarsen_communities as _size_coarsen\n370\t            # merge_small_communities is in metaqa_eval; use structural_encoder's version\n371\t            cm = _size_coarsen(G_und, cm, min_size=min_community_size)\n372\t            logger.info(\n373\t                \"%d communities after min_size=%d coarsening\",\n374\t              
  len(set(cm.values())), min_community_size,\n375\t            )\n376\t        elif coarsen_target is not None:\n377\t            cm = coarsen_communities(G_und, cm, target_max=coarsen_target)\n378\t            logger.info(\n379\t                \"%d communities after target_max=%d coarsening\",\n380\t                len(set(cm.values())), coarsen_target,\n381\t            )\n382\t\n383\t        self.adapter.community_map = cm\n384\t\n385\t        # ----------------------------------------------------------\n386\t        # 4. CSA engine\n387\t        # ----------------------------------------------------------\n388\t        logger.info(\"Building CSA engine...\")\n389\t        distances = build_community_distance_matrix(G_und, cm)\n390\t        adj       = adjacent_community_pairs(G_und, cm)\n391\t        cg        = build_community_graph(G_und, cm)\n392\t\n393\t        self._csa = CSAEngine(self.adapter)\n394\t        self._csa.set_community_graph(distances, adj, community_graph=cg)\n395\t\n396\t        # ----------------------------------------------------------\n397\t        # 5. BeamTraversal\n398\t        # ----------------------------------------------------------\n399\t        self._traversal = BeamTraversal(\n400\t            adapter             = self.adapter,\n401\t            csa_engine          = self._csa,\n402\t            beam_width          = self._beam_width,\n403\t            max_hop             = self._max_hop,\n404\t            max_neighbors       = self._max_neighbors,\n405\t            probabilistic       = self._probabilistic,\n406\t            warm_start_strength = self._warm_start_strength,\n407\t        )\n408\t\n409\t        self._built = True\n410\t        n_comm = len(set(cm.values()))\n411\t        logger.info(\n412\t            \"Build complete: %d nodes, %d edges, %d communities\",\n413\t            G.number_of_nodes(), G.number_of_edges(), n_comm,\n414\t        )\n415\t        return self\n416\t\n417\t    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n418\t    # CORTEX query\n419\t    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n420\t\n421\t    def query(\n422\t        self,\n423\t        seeds:             List[str],\n424\t        top_k:             int            = 10,\n425\t        min_hop:           int            = 1,\n426\t        max_hop:           Optional[int]  = None,\n427\t        query_embedding:   Optional[np.ndarray] = None,\n428\t        relation_prior=None,\n429\t        vote_weight:       float          = 0.30,\n430\t    ) -> List[Answer]:\n431\t        \"\"\"\n432\t        Traverse the graph from ``seeds`` and return ranked answers.\n433\t\n434\t        Parameters\n435\t        ----------\n436\t        seeds           : list of entity IDs to start from\n437\t        top_k           : number of answers to return\n438\t        min_hop         : minimum hop depth (1 = include 1-hop; 2 = exclude seeds)\n439\t        max_hop         : maximum traversal depth. Defaults to the value set at\n440\t                          build time. Override per-query to constrain depth (e.g.,\n441\t                          max_hop=1 for 1-hop eval avoids flooding with deep paths).\n442\t        query_embedding : optional query vector for semantic alignment\n443\t        relation_prior  : optional RelationPathPrior or GraphRelationPrior\n444\t        vote_weight     : convergence voting weight (default 0.30)\n445\t\n446\t        Returns\n447\t        -------\n448\t        List[Answer] sorted by score descending.\n449\t        \"\"\"\n450\t        if not self._built:\n451\t            raise RuntimeError(\n452\t                \"Graph has not been built. Call graph.build() first.\"\n453\t            )\n454\t\n455\t        # If caller requests a different max_hop than the built default, create\n456\t        # a lightweight traversal with the overridden depth for this query only.\n457\t        if max_hop is not None and max_hop != self._max_hop:\n458\t            traversal = BeamTraversal(\n459\t                adapter             = self.adapter,\n460\t                csa_engine          = self._csa,\n461\t                beam_width          = self._beam_width,\n462\t                max_hop             = max_hop,\n463\t                max_neighbors       = self._max_neighbors,\n464\t                probabilistic       = self._probabilistic,\n465\t                warm_start_strength = self._warm_start_strength,\n466\t          
  )\n467\t        else:\n468\t            traversal = self._traversal\n469\t\n470\t        paths = traversal.traverse(\n471\t            seeds,\n472\t            query_embedding=query_embedding,\n473\t        )\n474\t\n475\t        return extract(\n476\t            paths,\n477\t            top_k        = top_k,\n478\t            min_hop      = min_hop,\n479\t            query_embedding = query_embedding,\n480\t            relation_prior  = relation_prior,\n481\t            vote_weight     = vote_weight,\n482\t        )\n483\t\n484\t    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n485\t    # Convenience properties\n486\t    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n487\t\n488\t    @property\n489\t    def node_count(self) -> int:\n490\t        return self.adapter.node_count()\n491\t\n492\t    @property\n493\t    def edge_count(self) -> int:\n494\t        return self.adapter.to_networkx().number_of_edges()\n495\t\n496\t    @property\n497\t    def community_count(self) -> int:\n498\t        cm = getattr(self.adapter, \"community_map\", {})\n499\t        return len(set(cm.values()))\n500\t\n501\t    @property\n502\t    def is_built(self) -> bool:\n503\t        return self._built\n504\t\n505\t    def __repr__(self) -> str:\n506\t        status = \"built\" if self._built else \"not built\"\n507\t        return (\n508\t            f\"CerebrumGraph({self.node_count} nodes, \"\n509\t            f\"{self.edge_count} edges, \"\n510\t            f\"emb={type(self._embedding_engine).__name__}, \"\n511\t            f\"{status})\"\n512\t        )\n513\t\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"}]},"uuid":"3e880e0f-053d-4d9e-900f-0072d122faac","timestamp":"2026-04-01T16:13:14.414Z","toolUseResult":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"E:\\Development\\Cerebrum\\core\\cerebrum.py","content":"\"\"\"\nCerebrumGraph â€” unified THALAMUS â†’ CORTEX pipeline.\n\nThis is the main entry point for building and querying a knowledge graph\nwith CEREBRUM.  It replaces the manual wiring that previously lived inside\nindividual benchmark scripts.\n\nPipeline\n--------\nTHALAMUS (ingestion):\n  1. Load graph via adapter (from_kb / from_csv / from_triples / from_adapter)\n  2. Optional: apply provable completion rules (InverseRule, CompositionRule)\n  3. Encode entity embeddings (RandomEngine or SentenceEngine)\n  4. Detect communities (DSCF) and optionally coarsen\n  5. Build CSA attention engine with community distance matrix\n\nCORTEX (reasoning):\n  6. BeamTraversal over the CSA-weighted graph\n  7. AnswerExtractor with path scoring and convergence voting\n\nUsage\n-----\n    from core.cerebrum import CerebrumGraph\n    from core.graph_completion import InverseRule, CompositionRule\n\n    # Build from a pipe-separated triples file (MetaQA KB format)\n    graph = CerebrumGraph.from_kb(\n        \"benchmarks/data/metaqa/kb.txt\",\n        sep=\"|\",\n        embeddings=\"sentence\",\n    )\n\n    # Optional: augment with provable inference rules before building\n    graph.complete([\n        InverseRule(\"starred_actors\"),          # symmetric\n        InverseRule(\"directed_by\", \"director_of\"),\n    ])\n\n    # Run THALAMUS pipeline (embeddings + communities + CSA)\n    graph.build(cache_dir=\"cache/metaqa\", min_community_size=20)\n\n    # Query\n    answers = graph.query([\"Tom Hanks\"], top_k=10, max_hop=2, min_hop=1)\n    for a in answers:\n        print(a.entity_id, a.score, a.path_confidence)\n\"\"\"\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport logging\nimport pickle\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional, Union\n\nimport networkx as nx\nimport numpy as np\n\nfrom adapters.networkx_adapter import NetworkXAdapter\nfrom core.attention_engine import CSAEngine\nfrom core.embedding_engine import EmbeddingEngine, RandomEngine\nfrom core.graph_adapter import GraphAdapter\nfrom reasoning.answer_extractor import Answer, extract\nfrom reasoning.traversal import BeamTraversal\n\nlogger = logging.getLogger(\"cerebrum.graph\")\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# CerebrumGraph\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nclass CerebrumGraph:\n    \"\"\"\n    Encapsulates the full THALAMUS â†’ CORTEX pipeline for a single KG.\n\n    Parameters\n    ----------\n    adapter          : NetworkXAdapter (or any GraphAdapter) wrapping the graph\n    embedding_engine : EmbeddingEngine instance.  Defaults to RandomEngine(64).\n    beam_width       : default beam width for traversal (default 10)\n    max_hop          : maximum hop depth for traversal (default 3)\n    max_neighbors    : per-node neighbor expansion cap (default 100)\n    probabilistic    : enable Bayesian Beta-distribution path model (default False)\n    warm_start_strength : warm-start strength for probabilistic mode (default 0)\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        adapter:              GraphAdapter,\n        embedding_engine:     Optional[EmbeddingEngine] = None,\n        beam_width:           int   = 10,\n        max_hop:              int   = 3,\n        max_neighbors:        int   = 100,\n        probabilistic:        bool  = False,\n        warm_start_strength:  float = 0.0,\n    ):\n        self.adapter             = adapter\n        self._embedding_engine   = embedding_engine or RandomEngine(dim=64)\n        self._beam_width         = beam_width\n        self._max_hop            = max_hop\n        self._max_neighbors      = max_neighbors\n        self._probabilistic      = probabilistic\n        self._warm_start_strength = warm_start_strength\n\n        self._csa:       Optional[CSAEngine]    = None\n        self._traversal: Optional[BeamTraversal] = None\n        self._built      = False\n\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n    # Factory methods\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    @classmethod\n    def from_kb(\n        cls,\n        path:             Union[str, Path],\n        sep:              str  = \"|\",\n        directed:         bool = True,\n        embeddings:       str  = \"random\",\n        embedding_dim:    int  = 64,\n        pipeline=None,\n        **kwargs,\n    ) -> \"CerebrumGraph\":\n        \"\"\"\n        Load from a flat triples file (one triple per line).\n\n        Format:  head<sep>relation<sep>tail\n\n        Parameters\n        ----------\n        path          : path to the KB file\n        sep           : field separator (default \"|\" for MetaQA)\n        directed      : use DiGraph if True (default), Graph if False\n        embeddings    : \"random\" or \"sentence\"\n        embedding_dim : dimension for random embeddings (ignored for sentence)\n        pipeline      : optional IngestionPipeline for normalisation\n        \"\"\"\n        G = nx.DiGraph() if directed else nx.Graph()\n        with open(path, encoding=\"utf-8\") as f:\n            for line in f:\n                line = line.strip()\n                if not line or line.startswith(\"#\"):\n                    continue\n                parts = line.split(sep)\n            
    if len(parts) < 3:\n                    continue\n                s = parts[0].strip()\n                r = parts[1].strip()\n                o = parts[2].strip()\n                if not s or not r or not o:\n           
         continue\n\n                if pipeline is not None:\n                    edge = pipeline.process(s, o, r)\n                    G.add_edge(\n                        edge.source, edge.target,\n                       
 relation=edge.relation,\n                        confidence=float(edge.confidence),\n                        provenance=str(edge.provenance),\n                        weight=float(edge.weight),\n                    )\n                else:\n                    G.add_edge(s, o, relation=r, weight=1.0, confidence=1.0,\n                
               provenance=\"kb_file\")\n\n        adapter = NetworkXAdapter(G)\n        eng     = cls._make_engine(embeddings, embedding_dim)\n        return cls(adapter, embedding_engine=eng, **kwargs)\n\n    @classmethod\n    def from_csv(\n        cls,\n        path:          Union[str, Path],\n        embeddings:    str = \"random\",\n        embedding_dim: int = 64,\n        **kwargs,\n    ) -> \"CerebrumGraph\":\n        \"\"\"\n        Load from a CSV triples file (CEREBRUM CSV adapter format).\n        \"\"\"\n        from adapters.csv_adapter import load_csv_adapter\n        adapter = load_csv_adapter(str(path))\n        eng     = cls._make_engine(embeddings, embedding_dim)\n        return cls(adapter, embedding_engine=eng, **kwargs)\n\n    @classmethod\n    def from_triples(\n        cls,\n        triples:       List[tuple],\n        directed:      bool = True,\n        pipeline=None,\n        embeddings:    str  = \"random\",\n        embedding_dim: int  = 64,\n        **kwargs,\n    ) -> \"CerebrumGraph\":\n        \"\"\"\n        Build from a list of (subject, predicate, object[, meta_dict]) tuples.\n        \"\"\"\n        adapter = NetworkXAdapter.from_triples(triples, directed=directed,\n         
                                      pipeline=pipeline)\n        eng     = cls._make_engine(embeddings, embedding_dim)\n        return cls(adapter, embedding_engine=eng, **kwargs)\n\n    @classmethod\n    def from_adapter(\n        cls,\n        adapter:       GraphAdapter,\n        embeddings:    str = \"random\",\n        embedding_dim: int = 64,\n        **kwargs,\n    ) -> \"CerebrumGraph\":\n        \"\"\"Wrap an existing adapter (e.g. Neo4jAdapter, FederatedAdapter).\"\"\"\n        eng = cls._make_engine(embeddings, embedding_dim)\n        return cls(adapter, embedding_engine=eng, **kwargs)\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def _make_engine(embeddings: str, dim: int) -> EmbeddingEngine:\n        if embeddings == \"sentence\":\n            from core.embedding_engine import SentenceEngine\n            return SentenceEngine()\n        elif embeddings == \"random\":\n            return RandomEngine(dim=dim)\n        else:\n            raise ValueError(\n                f\"Unknown embeddings type {embeddings!r}. \"\n                \"Use 'random' or 'sentence'.\"\n            )\n\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n    # Provable graph completion (pre-build step)\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    def complete(self, rules: List[Any]) -> \"CerebrumGraph\":\n        \"\"\"\n        Apply provable inference rules to augment the graph before building.\n\n        Every rule adds synthetic edges with full provenance metadata.\n        No statistical predictions are made â€” only logical deductions.\n\n        Parameters\n        ----------\n        rules : list of CompletionRule instances\n                (InverseRule, CompositionRule, ...)\n\n        Returns self for chaining.\n        \"\"\"\n        total = 0\n        for rule in rules:\n            n = rule.apply(self.adapter)\n            logger.info(\"%s: +%d edges\", rule.describe(), n)\n            total += n\n        if total:\n            logger.info(\"graph_completion: %d synthetic edges added total\", total)\n        # Invalidate built state â€” must rebuild after completing\n        self._built = False\n        return self\n\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n    # THALAMUS build pipeline\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    def build(\n        self,\n        cache_dir:            Optional[Union[str, Path]] = None,\n        min_community_size:   int   = 0,\n        coarsen_target:       Optional[int] = None,\n        n_trials:             int   = 1,\n        seed:                 int   = 42,\n        force_rebuild:        bool  = False,\n    ) -> \"CerebrumGraph\":\n        \"\"\"\n        Run the THALAMUS pipeline: embeddings â†’ DSCF communities â†’ CSA.\n\n        Parameters\n        ----------\n        cache_dir           : directory for caching embeddings and communities.\n                              Pass None to skip caching.\n        min_community_size  : merge communities smaller than this (0 = disabled).\n                              Recommended: 20 for MetaQA.\n        coarsen_target      : alternatively, coarsen to at most this many\n                         
     communities (e.g. 300 for GrailQA).\n        n_trials            : DSCF runs to take the best of (default 1).\n        seed                : random seed for reproducibility.\n        force_rebuild       : ignore cache and recompute everything.\n\n        Returns self for chaining.\n        \"\"\"\n        from core.community_engine import dscf_communities\n        from core.structural_encoder import (\n            build_community_distance_matrix,\n            build_community_graph,\n            adjacent_community_pairs,\n            coarsen_communities,\n        )\n\n        cache = Path(cache_dir) if cache_dir else None\n        if cache:\n            cache.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n\n        G     = self.adapter.to_networkx()\n        nodes = list(G.nodes())\n\n        if not nodes:\n            raise ValueError(\"Graph has no nodes â€” nothing to build.\")\n\n        # ----------------------------------------------------------\n        # 1. Embeddings\n        # ----------------------------------------------------------\n        emb_cache = cache / \"embeddings.pkl\" if cache else None\n\n        if not force_rebuild and emb_cache and emb_cache.exists():\n            logger.info(\"Loading cached embeddings from %s\", emb_cache)\n            with open(emb_cache, \"rb\") as f:\n                self.adapter.embeddings = pickle.load(f)\n        else:\n            # Build label map: node_id â†’ human-readable label\n            entity_labels: Dict[str, str] = {}\n            for n in nodes:\n                data  = G.nodes[n]\n                label = data.get(\"label\") or data.get(\"friendly_name\") or str(n)\n                entity_labels[str(n)] = label\n\n            logger.info(\n                \"Encoding %d entities with %s\",\n              
  len(entity_labels),\n                type(self._embedding_engine).__name__,\n            )\n            self.adapter.embeddings = self._embedding_engine.encode_entities(\n                entity_labels\n            )\n\n            if emb_cache:\n                with open(emb_cache, \"wb\") as f:\n                    pickle.dump(self.adapter.embeddings, f)\n\n        # ----------------------------------------------------------\n        # 2. Community detection (DSCF)\n        # ----------------------------------------------------------\n        comm_cache = cache / \"communities.pkl\" if cache else None\n        G_und      = G.to_undirected() if G.is_directed() else G\n\n        if not force_rebuild and comm_cache and comm_cache.exists():\n            logger.info(\"Loading cached communities from %s\", comm_cache)\n            with open(comm_cache, \"rb\") as f:\n                parts = pickle.load(f)\n        else:\n            logger.info(\n                \"Running DSCF on %d nodes, %d edges (n_trials=%d)...\",\n                G.number_of_nodes(), G.number_of_edges(), n_trials,\n            )\n            if n_trials > 1:\n                from core.community_engine import best_of_n_dscf\n                parts = best_of_n_dscf(G_und, n_trials=n_trials, seed=seed)\n            else:\n                parts = dscf_communities(G_und)\n\n            if comm_cache:\n                with open(comm_cache, \"wb\") as f:\n                
    pickle.dump(parts, f)\n\n        self.adapter._partition = parts\n        cm: Dict = {n: cid for cid, members in enumerate(parts) for n in members}\n        n_raw = len(set(cm.values()))\n        logger.info(\"%d raw DSCF communities\", n_raw)\n\n        # ----------------------------------------------------------\n        # 3. Optional coarsening\n        # ----------------------------------------------------------\n        if min_community_size > 0:\n            from core.structural_encoder import coarsen_communities as _size_coarsen\n            # merge_small_communities is in metaqa_eval; use structural_encoder's version\n            cm = _size_coarsen(G_und, cm, min_size=min_community_size)\n            logger.info(\n                \"%d communities after min_size=%d coarsening\",\n                len(set(cm.values())), min_community_size,\n            )\n        elif coarsen_target is not None:\n            cm = coarsen_communities(G_und, cm, target_max=coarsen_target)\n            logger.info(\n                \"%d communities after target_max=%d coarsening\",\n                len(set(cm.values())), coarsen_target,\n            )\n\n        self.adapter.community_map = cm\n\n        # ----------------------------------------------------------\n        # 4. CSA engine\n        # ----------------------------------------------------------\n        logger.info(\"Building CSA engine...\")\n        distances = build_community_distance_matrix(G_und, cm)\n        adj       = adjacent_community_pairs(G_und, cm)\n        cg        = build_community_graph(G_und, cm)\n\n        self._csa = CSAEngine(self.adapter)\n        self._csa.set_community_graph(distances, adj, community_graph=cg)\n\n        # ----------------------------------------------------------\n        # 5. BeamTraversal\n        # ----------------------------------------------------------\n        self._traversal = BeamTraversal(\n            adapter             = self.adapter,\n            csa_engine          = self._csa,\n            beam_width          = self._beam_width,\n            max_hop             = self._max_hop,\n            max_neighbors       = self._max_neighbors,\n            probabilistic       = self._probabilistic,\n            warm_start_strength = self._warm_start_strength,\n        )\n\n        self._built = True\n        n_comm = len(set(cm.values()))\n        logger.info(\n            \"Build complete: %d nodes, %d edges, %d communities\",\n            G.number_of_nodes(), G.number_of_edges(), n_comm,\n        )\n        return self\n\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n    # CORTEX query\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    def query(\n        self,\n        seeds:             List[str],\n        top_k:             int            = 10,\n        min_hop:           int            = 1,\n        max_hop:           Optional[int]  = None,\n        query_embedding:   Optional[np.ndarray] = None,\n        relation_prior=None,\n        vote_weight:       float          = 0.30,\n    ) -> List[Answer]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Traverse the graph from ``seeds`` and return ranked answers.\n\n        Parameters\n        ----------\n        seeds           : list of entity IDs to start from\n        top_k           : number of answers to return\n        min_hop         : minimum hop depth (1 = include 1-hop; 2 = exclude seeds)\n        max_hop         : maximum traversal depth. Defaults to the value set at\n                          build time. Override per-query to constrain depth (e.g.,\n                          max_hop=1 for 1-hop eval avoids flooding with deep paths).\n        query_embedding : optional query vector for semantic alignment\n        relation_prior  : optional RelationPathPrior or GraphRelationPrior\n        vote_weight     : convergence voting weight (default 0.30)\n\n        Returns\n        -------\n        List[Answer] sorted by score descending.\n        \"\"\"\n        if not self._built:\n            raise RuntimeError(\n                \"Graph has not been built. Call graph.build() first.\"\n            )\n\n        # If caller requests a different max_hop than the built default, create\n        # a lightweight traversal with the overridden depth for this query only.\n        if max_hop is not None and max_hop != self._max_hop:\n            traversal = BeamTraversal(\n                adapter             = self.adapter,\n                csa_engine          = self._csa,\n                beam_width          = self._beam_width,\n                max_hop             = max_hop,\n                max_neighbors       = self._max_neighbors,\n                probabilistic       = self._probabilistic,\n                warm_start_strength = self._warm_start_strength,\n            )\n        else:\n            traversal = self._traversal\n\n        paths = traversal.traverse(\n            seeds,\n            query_embedding=query_embedding,\n        )\n\n        return extract(\n            paths,\n            top_k        = top_k,\n            min_hop      = min_hop,\n            query_embedding = query_embedding,\n            relation_prior  = relation_prior,\n            vote_weight     = vote_weight,\n        )\n\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n    # Convenience properties\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    @property\n    def node_count(self) -> int:\n        return self.adapter.node_count()\n\n    @property\n    def edge_count(self) -> int:\n        return self.adapter.to_networkx().number_of_edges()\n\n    @property\n    def community_count(self) -> int:\n        cm = getattr(self.adapter, \"community_map\", {})\n        return len(set(cm.values()))\n\n    @property\n    def is_built(self) -> bool:\n        return self._built\n\n    def __repr__(self) -> str:\n        status = \"built\" if self._built else \"not built\"\n        return (\n            f\"CerebrumGraph({self.node_count} nodes, \"\n            f\"{self.edge_count} edges, \"\n            f\"emb={type(self._embedding_engine).__name__}, \"\n            f\"{status})\"\n        )\n","numLines":513,"startLine":1,"totalLines":513}},"sourceToolAssistantUUID":"c8c9c19d-1652-4462-b388-ec3f7af008f2","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"E:\\Development\\Cerebrum","sessionId":"1b39a2ba-e177-466c-ade0-cb0f0629b3b0","version":"2.1.87","gitBranch":"main","slug":"dynamic-marinating-meadow"}
