del adapter._ngram_index\n\n        return new\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# GeneralizedCompositionRule\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nclass GeneralizedCompositionRule(CompletionRule):\n    \"\"\"\n    Auto-discover and materialize ALL composable 2-hop relation pairs in the\n    graph without requiring manual (R1, R2) specification.\n\n    For every intermediate node B that has at least one incoming edge with\n    relation R1 and at least one outgoing edge with relation R2, materialize\n    a shortcut A -\"R1|R2\"-> C for every matching (A, B, C) triple.\n\n    Only relation pairs that appear in at least ``min_occurrences`` distinct\n    (A, C) pairs are materialized (corroborating-evidence filter).\n\n    This is a *generalization* of CompositionRule: it is equivalent to running\n    CompositionRule for every (R1, R2) pair in the graph, but does so in a\n    single efficient pass without needing explicit enumeration.\n\n    Logical basis\n    -------------\n    Every synthetic edge is backed by a concrete 2-hop path (A, B, C) that\n    already exists in the graph.  The composed relation name encodes exactly\n    which two relations were chained, preserving full traceability.\n\n    Parameters\n    ----------\n    min_occurrences : minimum number of distinct (A, C) pairs that must be\n                      reachable via a given (R1, R2) pair before edges for\n                   
   that pair are materialized (default 2)\n    max_edges       : safety cap on total edges added (default 1_000_000)\n\n    Provenance\n    ----------\n    Every synthetic edge records:\n        provenance  = \"rule:compose_auto:{R1}+{R2}|path:{A}â†’{B}â†’{C}\"\n        relation    = \"{R1}|{R2}\"\n        synthetic   = True\n        confidence  = min(conf_ab, conf_bc)\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        min_occurrences: int = 2,\n        max_edges:       int = 1_000_000,\n    ):\n        self.min_occurrences = min_occurrences\n        self.max_edges       = max_edges\n\n    def describe(self) -> str:\n        return (\n            f\"GeneralizedCompositionRule: auto-discover all R1+R2 pairs \"\n            f\"(min_occurrences={self.min_occurrences}, \"\n            f\"max_edges={self.max_edges:,})\"\n        )\n\n    def apply(self, adapter: NetworkXAdapter) -> int:  # noqa: C901\n        from collections import defaultdict\n\n        G   = adapter._G\n        new = 0\n\n        # pair_evidence[(R1, R2)][(A, C)] = (best_b, best_conf, best_prov)\n        # We accumulate evidence per (R1,R2) pair, then filter by min_occurrences.\n        pair_evidence: Dict[\n            Tuple[str, str],\n            Dict[Tuple[str, str], Tuple[str, float, str]],\n        ] = defaultdict(dict)\n\n        directed = G.is_directed()\n\n        for b in G.nodes():\n            # Incoming edges A â†’ B\n            in_edges  = list(G.in_edges(b, data=True)) if directed \\\n                        else [(nb, b, d) for nb, d in G[b].items()\n                              for d in ([d] if isinstance(d, dict) else d.values())]\n            # Outgoing edges B â†’ C\n            out_edges = list(G.out_edges(b, data=True)) if directed \\\n                        else [(b, nb, d) for nb, d in G[b].items()\n                              for d in ([d] if isinstance(d, dict) else d.values())]\n\n            if not in_edges or not out_edges:\n                continue\n\n            for a, _b, d_ab in in_edges:\n                r1 = d_ab.get(\"relation\")\n                if not r1:\n                    continue\n                conf_ab = float(d_ab.get(\"confidence\", 1.0))\n\n                for _b2, c, d_bc in out_edges:\n                    if c == a:          # skip trivial self-loops\n                        continue\n                    r2 = d_bc.get(\"relation\")\n                    if not r2:\n                        continue\n                    conf_bc = float(d_bc.get(\"confidence\", 1.0))\n                    conf    = min(conf_ab, conf_bc)\n                    key     = (r1, r2)\n                    ac      = (a, c)\n                    prov    = (\n                        f\"rule:compose_auto:{r1}+{r2}\"\n                        f\"|path:{a}\\u2192{b}\\u2192{c}\"\n                    )\n\n                    existing = pair_evidence[key].get(ac)\n           
         if existing is None or conf > existing[1]:\n                        pair_evidence[key][ac] = (b, conf, prov)\n\n        # Materialize edges for qualifying pairs\n        to_add: List[Tuple] = []\n        for (r1, r2), ac_map in pair_evidence.items():\n            if len(ac_map) < self.min_occurrences:\n                continue\n            composed_rel = f\"{r1}|{r2}\"\n            for (a, c), (_b, conf, prov) in ac_map.items():\n                if G.has_edge(a, c):\n                    existing = G.get_edge_data(a, c) or {}\n                   
 if existing.get(\"relation\") == composed_rel:\n                        continue\n                to_add.append((\n                    a,\n                    c,\n                    {\n                        \"relation\":   composed_rel,\n                        \"weight\":     conf,\n                        \"confidence\": conf,\n                        \"provenance\": prov,\n                        \"synthetic\":  True,\n                  
  },\n                ))\n                if len(to_add) >= self.max_edges:\n                    break\n            if len(to_add) >= self.max_edges:\n                logger.warning(\n                    \"GeneralizedCompositionRule: hit max_edges cap %d\",\n                    self.max_edges,\n                )\n                break\n\n        for a, c, attrs in to_add:\n            G.add_edge(a, c, **attrs)\n            new += 1\n\n        if new:\n            logger.debug(\"GeneralizedCompositionRule: added %d edges\", new)\n            if hasattr(adapter, \"_ngram_index\"):\n                del adapter._ngram_index\n\n        return new"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":"tool_use","stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":10039,"cache_read_input_tokens":34014,"output_tokens":2114,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":10039,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[],"speed":"standard"}},"requestId":"req_011CZdM54avmxpxM9Gjw8x6k","type":"assistant","uuid":"5ae46354-7912-4c2a-b586-e351aebecb5f","timestamp":"2026-04-01T16:13:47.395Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"E:\\Development\\Cerebrum","sessionId":"1b39a2ba-e177-466c-ade0-cb0f0629b3b0","version":"2.1.87","gitBranch":"main","slug":"dynamic-marinating-meadow"}

The composed relation name encodes exactly\n    which two relations were chained, preserving full traceability.\n\n    Parameters\n    ----------\n    min_occurrences : minimum number of distinct (A, C) pairs that must be\n                      reachable via a given (R1, R2) pair before edges for\n                      that pair are materialized (default 2)\n    max_edges       : safety cap on total edges added (default 1_000_000)\n\n    Provenance\n    ----------\n    Every synthetic edge records:\n        provenance  = \"rule:compose_auto:{R1}+{R2}|path:{A}â†’{B}â†’{C}\"\n        relation    = \"{R1}|{R2}\"\n        synthetic   = True\n        confidence  = min(conf_ab, conf_bc)\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        min_occurrences: int = 2,\n        max_edges:       int = 1_000_000,\n    ):\n        self.min_occurrences = min_occurrences\n        self.max_edges       = max_edges\n\n    def describe(self) -> str:\n        return (\n            f\"GeneralizedCompositionRule: auto-discover all R1+R2 pairs \"\n            f\"(min_occurrences={self.min_occurrences}, \"\n            f\"max_edges={self.max_edges:,})\"\n        )\n\n    def apply(self, adapter: NetworkXAdapter) -> int:  # noqa: C901\n        from collections import defaultdict\n\n        G   = adapter._G\n        new = 0\n\n        # pair_evidence[(R1, R2)][(A, C)] = (best_b, best_conf, best_prov)\n        # We accumulate evidence per (R1,R2) pair, then filter by min_occurrences.\n        pair_evidence: Dict[\n            Tuple[str, str],\n            Dict[Tuple[str, str], Tuple[str, float, str]],\n        ] = defaultdict(dict)\n\n        directed = G.is_directed()\n\n        for b in G.nodes():\n            # Incoming edges A â†’ B\n            in_edges  = list(G.in_edges(b, data=True)) if directed \\\n                        else [(nb, b, d) for nb, d in G[b].items()\n                              for d in ([d] if isinstance(d, dict) else d.values())]\n            # Outgoing edges B â†’ C\n            out_edges = list(G.out_edges(b, data=True)) if directed \\\n                        else [(b, nb, d) for nb, d in G[b].items()\n                              for d in ([d] if isinstance(d, dict) else d.values())]\n\n            if not in_edges or not out_edges:\n          
      continue\n\n            for a, _b, d_ab in in_edges:\n                r1 = d_ab.get(\"relation\")\n                if not r1:\n                    continue\n                conf_ab = float(d_ab.get(\"confidence\", 1.0))\n\n                for _b2, c, d_bc in out_edges:\n                    if c == a:          # skip trivial self-loops\n                        continue\n                    r2 = d_bc.get(\"relation\")\n                    if not r2:\n                        continue\n                    conf_bc = float(d_bc.get(\"confidence\", 1.0))\n                    conf    = min(conf_ab, conf_bc)\n                    key     = (r1, r2)\n                  
  ac      = (a, c)\n                    prov    = (\n                        f\"rule:compose_auto:{r1}+{r2}\"\n                        f\"|path:{a}\\u2192{b}\\u2192{c}\"\n                    )\n\n                    existing = pair_evidence[key].get(ac)\n                    if existing is None or conf > existing[1]:\n                 
       pair_evidence[key][ac] = (b, conf, prov)\n\n        # Materialize edges for qualifying pairs\n        to_add: List[Tuple] = []\n        for (r1, r2), ac_map in pair_evidence.items():\n            if len(ac_map) < self.min_occurrences:\n                continue\n            composed_rel = f\"{r1}|{r2}\"\n            for (a, c), (_b, conf, prov) in ac_map.items():\n                if G.has_edge(a, c):\n                    existing = G.get_edge_data(a, c) or {}\n                    if existing.get(\"relation\") == composed_rel:\n                     
   continue\n                to_add.append((\n                    a,\n                    c,\n                  
  {\n                        \"relation\":   composed_rel,\n                        \"weight\":     conf,\n                        \"confidence\": conf,\n                        \"provenance\": prov,\n                       
 \"synthetic\":  True,\n                    },\n                ))\n                if len(to_add) >= self.max_edges:\n                    break\n            if len(to_add) >= self.max_edges:\n                logger.warning(\n                    \"GeneralizedCompositionRule: hit max_edges cap %d\",\n                    self.max_edges,\n                )\n                break\n\n        for a, c, attrs in to_add:\n            G.add_edge(a, c, **attrs)\n            new += 1\n\n        if new:\n            logger.debug(\"GeneralizedCompositionRule: added %d edges\", new)\n            if hasattr(adapter, \"_ngram_index\"):\n                del adapter._ngram_index\n\n        return new","originalFile":"\"\"\"\nProvable graph completion rules for CEREBRUM.\n\nRules add synthetic edges to the graph with full provenance metadata.\nEvery synthetic edge cites the specific rule and backing evidence that\nproduced it.  No statistical predictions are made â€” only logical deductions.\n\nAvailable rules\n---------------\nInverseRule\n    Add B -R_inv-> A whenever A -R-> B exists, for a known symmetric or\n    inverse-relation pair.  Backed by the source edge.\n\nCompositionRule\n    Add A -R_composed-> C whenever A -R1-> B -R2-> C exists, for a known\n    composable relation pair.  Backed by the two-hop path (A,B,C).\n\nAll synthetic edges carry:\n  relation    : the logical relation name\n  synthetic   : True\n  confidence  : min(conf(backing edges))   [weakest-link rule]\n  provenance  : structured string citing the rule and backing evidence\n  weight      : min(weight(backing edges))\n\nProvenenance format\n-------------------\n  InverseRule:\n    \"rule:inverse:{R}â†’{R_inv}|source:{src_id}â†’{tgt_id}\"\n\n  CompositionRule:\n    \"rule:compose:{R1}+{R2}â†’{Rc}|path:{a}â†’{b}â†’{c}\"\n\"\"\"\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport logging\nfrom typing import Dict, List, Optional, Set, Tuple\n\nimport networkx as nx\n\nfrom adapters.networkx_adapter import NetworkXAdapter\n\nlogger = logging.getLogger(\"cerebrum.graph_completion\")\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Base class\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nclass CompletionRule:\n    \"\"\"Abstract base for provable graph completion rules.\"\"\"\n\n    def apply(self, adapter: NetworkXAdapter) -> int:\n        \"\"\"\n        Apply this rule to the adapter's underlying graph.\n\n        Returns the number of new edges added.\n        \"\"\"\n        raise NotImplementedError\n\n    def describe(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Human-readable description of what this rule does.\"\"\"\n        raise NotImplementedError\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# InverseRule\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nclass InverseRule(CompletionRule):\n    \"\"\"\n    Materialize the inverse (or symmetric twin) of a known relation.\n\n    For symmetric relations (e.g. MARRIED_TO):\n        InverseRule(\"MARRIED_TO\")\n        â†’ adds B -MARRIED_TO-> A for every A -MARRIED_TO-> B\n\n    For asymmetric inverse pairs (e.g. directed_by / director_of):\n        InverseRule(\"directed_by\", \"director_of\")\n        â†’ adds B -director_of-> A for every A -directed_by-> B\n\n    Logical basis\n    -------------\n    The rule encodes a domain fact about the relation's semantics â€” not a\n    statistical guess.  The caller is asserting that the relation is known\n    to be symmetric (or to have a named inverse), which is a statement about\n    the ontology, not about the data.\n\n    Provenance\n    ----------\n    Every synthetic edge records:\n        provenance = \"rule:inverse:{relation}â†’{inverse}|source:{src}â†’{tgt}\"\n        confidence = source edge confidence\n        synthetic  = True\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, relation: str, inverse_relation: Optional[str] = None):\n        \"\"\"\n        Parameters\n        ----------\n        relation         : the forward relation to match\n        inverse_relation : the relation name for the reverse edge.\n                           Defaults to ``relation`` (symmetric case).\n        \"\"\"\n        self.relation         = relation\n        self.inverse_relation = inverse_relation or relation\n\n    def describe(self) -> str:\n        if self.inverse_relation == self.relation:\n            return f\"InverseRule: {self.relation!r} is symmetric â†’ add reverse edges\"\n        return (\n            f\"InverseRule: {self.relation!r} â†’ \"\n            f\"inverse is {self.inverse_relation!r}\"\n        )\n\n    def apply(self, adapter: NetworkXAdapter) -> int:\n        G   = adapter._G\n        new = 0\n\n        # Collect edges to add (don't mutate while iterating)\n        to_add: List[Tuple] = []\n        for src, tgt, data in list(G.edges(data=True)):\n            if data.get(\"relation\") != self.relation:\n                continue\n            # Skip if the inverse already exists\n            if G.has_edge(tgt, src):\n                existing = G.get_edge_data(tgt, src) or {}\n                if existing.get(\"relation\") == self.inverse_relation:\n                    continue\n\n            provenance = (\n                f\"rule:inverse:{self.relation}â†’{self.inverse_relation}\"\n              
  f\"|source:{src}â†’{tgt}\"\n            )\n            to_add.append((\n                tgt,\n                
src,\n                {\n                    \"relation\":   self.inverse_relation,\n                    \"weight\":     float(data.get(\"weight\", 1.0)),\n                    \"confidence\": float(data.get(\"confidence\", 1.0)),\n                    \"provenance\": provenance,\n                    \"synthetic\":  True,\n             
       \"valid_from\": data.get(\"valid_from\"),\n                    \"valid_to\":   data.get(\"valid_to\"),\n                },\n            ))\n\n        for tgt, src, attrs in to_add:\n            G.add_edge(tgt, src, **attrs)\n            new += 1\n\n        if new:\n            logger.debug(\n                \"InverseRule(%r â†’ %r): added %d edges\",\n                self.relation, self.inverse_relation, new,\n            )\n            
# Invalidate n-gram index if present\n            if hasattr(adapter, \"_ngram_index\"):\n                del adapter._ngram_index\n\n        return new\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# CompositionRule\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nclass CompositionRule(CompletionRule):\n    \"\"\"\n    Materialize a composed relation A -Rc-> C whenever A -R1-> B -R2-> C exists.\n\n    Example: if R1=\"directed_by\" and R2=\"born_in\", then composing them\n    produces A -directed_by:born_in-> C meaning \"the director of A was born\n    in C\".  Give it a meaningful name via ``composed_relation``.\n\n    Logical basis\n    -------------\n    The synthetic edge is not a guess â€” it is a shortcut backed by the\n    two-hop path (A, B, C) that already exists in the graph.  The path is\n    cited in the provenance of every added edge.  If the backing path is\n    ever removed, the synthetic edge loses its citation but can be recomputed.\n\n    This is identical to what a query engine would compute on-the-fly for a\n    two-hop SPARQL join; materializing it makes it traversable in one hop.\n\n    Parameters\n    ----------\n    r1                : first relation in the chain\n    r2                : second relation in the chain\n    composed_relation : name for the materialized shortcut edge\n    min_occurrences   : only materialize if this (A, C) pair is reached by\n                        at least ``min_occurrences`` distinct intermediate B\n                
        nodes.  Default 1 (add on any single evidence path).\n                        Raise to 2+ to require corroborating evidence.\n    max_edges         : safety cap on total edges added (default 500_000)\n\n    Provenance\n    ----------\n    Every synthetic edge records:\n        provenance = \"rule:compose:{R1}+{R2}â†’{Rc}|path:{a}â†’{b}â†’{c}\"\n                     (first supporting path only; confidence = weakest-link)\n        synthetic  = True\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        r1:                str,\n        r2:                str,\n        composed_relation: str,\n        min_occurrences:   int = 1,\n        max_edges:         int = 500_000,\n    ):\n        self.r1                = r1\n        self.r2                = r2\n        self.composed_relation = composed_relation\n        self.min_occurrences   = min_occurrences\n        self.max_edges         = max_edges\n\n    def describe(self) -> str:\n        return (\n            f\"CompositionRule: {self.r1!r} + {self.r2!r} â†’ \"\n            f\"{self.composed_relation!r}  \"\n            f\"(min_occurrences={self.min_occurrences})\"\n        )\n\n    def apply(self, adapter: NetworkXAdapter) -> int:\n        G   = adapter._G\n        new = 0\n\n        # Index: A â†’ {B nodes reachable via R1}\n        # Then for each B, follow R2 to find C nodes.\n        # Group by (A, C) to count distinct intermediates.\n\n        # intermediate_data[(a, c)] = list of (b, conf_ab, conf_bc, prov_path)\n        from collections import defaultdict\n        evidence: Dict[\n            Tuple[str, str],\n            List[Tuple[str, float, float, str]]\n        ] = defaultdict(list)\n\n        for a, b, d_ab in G.edges(data=True):\n            if d_ab.get(\"relation\") != self.r1:\n            
    continue\n            conf_ab = float(d_ab.get(\"confidence\", 1.0))\n\n            for _b, c, d_bc in G.out_edges(b, data=True) if G.is_directed() \\\n                    else G.edges(b, data=True):\n                if _b != b:\n                    continue\n                if d_bc.get(\"relation\") != self.r2:\n                 
   continue\n                if c == a:          # skip trivial cycles\n                    continue\n          
      conf_bc = float(d_bc.get(\"confidence\", 1.0))\n                prov    = (\n                    f\"rule:compose:{self.r1}+{self.r2}â†’{self.composed_relation}\"\n                    f\"|path:{a}â†’{b}â†’{c}\"\n                )\n                evidence[(a, c)].append((b, conf_ab, conf_bc, prov))\n\n        to_add: List[Tuple] = []\n        for (a, c), paths in evidence.items():\n            if len(paths) < self.min_occurrences:\n                continue\n            if G.has_edge(a, c):\n                existing = G.get_edge_data(a, c) or {}\n                if existing.get(\"relation\") == self.composed_relation:\n                    continue\n\n         
   # Use the highest-confidence path as the primary citation\n            best = max(paths, key=lambda x: min(x[1], x[2]))\n            b_best, conf_ab, conf_bc, prov = best\n            confidence = min(conf_ab, conf_bc)\n\n            to_add.append((\n                a,\n                c,\n                {\n                    \"relation\":   self.composed_relation,\n                    \"weight\":     confidence,\n                    \"confidence\": confidence,\n                    \"provenance\": prov,\n                    \"synthetic\":  True,\n                },\n            ))\n\n            if len(to_add) >= self.max_edges:\n                logger.warning(\n                    \"CompositionRule(%r+%r): hit max_edges cap %d\",\n                    self.r1, self.r2, self.max_edges,\n                )\n                break\n\n        for a, c, attrs in to_add:\n            G.add_edge(a, c, **attrs)\n            new += 1\n\n        if new:\n            logger.debug(\n                \"CompositionRule(%r+%r â†’ %r): added %d edges\",\n                self.r1, self.r2, self.composed_relation, new,\n            )\n            if hasattr(adapter, \"_ngram_index\"):\n                del adapter._ngram_index\n\n        return new\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":304,"oldLines":3,"newStart":304,"newLines":156,"lines":["                 del ---------------------------------------------------------------------------","+","+class GeneralizedCompositionRule(CompletionRule):","+    \"\"\"","+    Auto-discover and materialize ALL composable 2-hop relation pairs in the","+    graph without requiring manual (R1, R2) specification.","+","+    For every intermediate node B that has at least one incoming edge with","+    relation R1 and at least one outgoing edge with relation R2, materialize","+    a shortcut A -\"R1|R2\"-> C for every matching (A, B, C) triple.","+","+    Only relation pairs that appear in at least ``min_occurrences`` distinct","+    (A, C) pairs are materialized (corroborating-evidence filter).","+","+    This is a *generalization* of CompositionRule: it is equivalent to running","+    CompositionRule for every (R1, R2) pair in the graph, but does so in a","+    single efficient pass without needing explicit enumeration.","+","+    Logical basis","+    -------------","+    Every synthetic edge is backed by a concrete 2-hop path (A, B, C) that","+    already exists in the graph.  The composed relation name encodes exactly","+    which two relations were chained, preserving full traceability.","+","+    Parameters","+    ----------","+    min_occurrences : minimum number of distinct (A, C) pairs that must be","+                      reachable via a given (R1, R2) pair before edges for","+                      that pair are materialized (default 2)","+    max_edges       : safety cap on total edges added (default 1_000_000)","+","+    Provenance","+    ----------","+    Every synthetic edge records:","+        provenance  = \"rule:compose_auto:{R1}+{R2}|path:{A}â†’{B}â†’{C}\"","+        relation    = \"{R1}|{R2}\"","+        synthetic   = True","+        confidence  = min(conf_ab, conf_bc)","+    \"\"\"","+","+    def __init__(","+        self,","+        min_occurrences: int = 2,","+        max_edges:       int = 1_000_000,","+    ):","+        self.min_occurrences = min_occurrences","+        self.max_edges       = max_edges","+","+    def describe(self) -> str:","+        return (","+            f\"GeneralizedCompositionRule: auto-discover all R1+R2 pairs \"","+            f\"(min_occurrences={self.min_occurrences}, \"","+            f\"max_edges={self.max_edges:,})\"","+        )","+","+    def apply(self, adapter: NetworkXAdapter) -> int:  # Outgoing edges B â†’ C","+            out_edges = list(G.out_edges(b, data=True)) if directed \\","+                    
    else [(b, nb, d) for nb, d in G[b].items()","+                              for d in ([d] if isinstance(d, dict) else d.values())]","+","+            if not in_edges or not out_edges:","+                continue","+","+            for a, _b, d_ab in in_edges:","+                r1 = d_ab.get(\"relation\")","+                if not r1:","+                    continue","+                conf_ab = float(d_ab.get(\"confidence\", 1.0))","+","+                for _b2, c, d_bc in out_edges:","+                    if c == a:          # skip trivial self-loops","+                        continue","+                    r2 = d_bc.get(\"relation\")","+                    
if not r2:","+                        continue","+                    conf_bc = float(d_bc.get(\"confidence\", 1.0))","+                    conf    = min(conf_ab, conf_bc)","+                    key     = (r1, r2)","+                    ac      = (a, c)","+                    prov    = (","+                        f\"rule:compose_auto:{r1}+{r2}\"","+                        f\"|path:{a}\\u2192{b}\\u2192{c}\"","+                    )","+","+                    existing = pair_evidence[key].get(ac)","+                    if existing is None or conf > existing[1]:","+                        pair_evidence[key][ac] = (b, conf, prov)","+","+        # Materialize edges for qualifying pairs","+        to_add: List[Tuple] = []","+        for (r1, r2), ac_map in pair_evidence.items():","+            if len(ac_map) < self.min_occurrences:","+                continue","+            composed_rel = f\"{r1}|{r2}\"","+            for (a, c), (_b, conf, prov) in ac_map.items():","+                if G.has_edge(a, c):","+                    existing = G.get_edge_data(a, c) or {}","+                    if existing.get(\"relation\") == composed_rel:","+                        continue","+                to_add.append((","+

   },","+                ))","+                if len(to_add) >= self.max_edges:","+                    break","+            if len(to_add) >= self.max_edges:","+                logger.warning(","+                    \"GeneralizedCompositionRule: hit max_edges cap %d\",","+                    self.max_edges,","+                )","+                break","+","+        for a, c, attrs in to_add:","+            G.add_edge(a, c, **attrs)","+            new += 1","+","+        if new:","+            logger.debug(\"GeneralizedCompositionRule: added %d edges\", new)","+            if hasattr(adapter, \"_ngram_index\"):","+                del adapter._ngram_index","+","+        return new"]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false},"sourceToolAssistantUUID":"5ae46354-7912-4c2a-b586-e351aebecb5f","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"E:\\Development\\Cerebrum","sessionId":"1b39a2ba-e177-466c-ade0-cb0f0629b3b0","version":"2.1.87","gitBranch":"main","slug":"dynamic-marinating-meadow"}

{"parentUuid":"2108c6c2-25e5-4f5e-83f6-633462cb4d0f","isSidechain":false,"message":{"model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","id":"msg_0188a6y1xkkHFMr5mytEpcXt","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01KBKQDjenvWANeWBNWKhH3a","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"E:\\Development\\Cerebrum\\core\\graph_bridge.py","content":"\"\"\"\nGraphBridgeEngine â€” proactive cross-component link synthesis for CEREBRUM.\n\nThis module implements the second layer of the Enhancement Layer:\n\n  THALAMUS â†’ [complete()] â†’ [enhance()] â†’ build() â†’ CORTEX\n\nProblem\n-------\nCWQ and similar KG benchmarks contain per-question 2-hop subgraphs that were\nextracted independently.  When merged into a single scaffold, entities that\nappear in *different* questions but refer to the same or semantically related\nconcepts end up in disconnected graph components.  DSCF therefore produces\nhundreds of thousands of isolated communities, and BeamTraversal cannot cross\ncomponent boundaries â€” resulting in near-zero multi-hop recall.\n\nSolution\n--------\nGraphBridgeEngine is a *proactive* bridge synthesizer that runs BEFORE\nBeamTraversal.  It:\n\n  1. Detects connected components in the graph.\n  2. Identifies \"frontier\" nodes â€” low-degree nodes on the periphery of small\n     components that are likely missing inter-component links.\n  3. Uses pre-trained SentenceEngine embeddings (fixed, not trained on task\n     data) to find pairs of frontier nodes in *different* components whose\n     cosine similarity exceeds ``min_similarity``.\n  4. Materializes a bridge edge with a glass-box provenance string that records\n     the similarity score, the source/target frontier nodes, and the component\n     IDs being connected.\n\nEvery synthetic edge is fully traceable:\n    provenance = \"rule:bridge_embed|sim:{s:.4f}|from:{F}|to:{N}|components:{c1}->{c2}\"\n\nThis is NOT link prediction in the ML sense â€” no model is trained on the task.\nSentenceEngine is used purely as a fixed semantic projector, identical to how\nit encodes entities during the standard build() pipeline.\n\nCrystal- vs Glass-box classification\n-------------------------------------\n  CompositionRule / GeneralizedCompositionRule = Crystal-box\n    (purely logical, no numerical thresholds, zero uncertainty)\n  GraphBridgeEngine = Glass-box\n    (transparent algorithm + confidence scores; uses a pre-trained, frozen\n    embedding model as a fixed distance metric â€” no task-specific training)\n\nParameters\n----------\nmin_similarity    : cosine similarity threshold for creating a bridge edge\n                    (default 0.80 â€” empirically tuned for Freebase name strings)\ntop_k             : maximum bridges per frontier node (default 5)\nmax_degree        : maximum degree for a node to qualify as a frontier node\n                    (default 2 â€” keeps only genuinely peripheral nodes)\nmax_bridges       : hard cap on total bridge edges added (default 500_000)\nmax_frontier_nodes: maximum frontier nodes to embed (default 10_000).\n                    Nodes are sampled from components with 1â€“50 nodes.\nbatch_size        : embedding batch size (default 512)\n\nUsage\n-----\n    from core.graph_bridge import GraphBridgeEngine\n    bridge_engine = GraphBridgeEngine(min_similarity=0.82, top_k=3)\n    n_added = bridge_engine.apply(adapter, embedding_engine)\n\"\"\"\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport logging\nimport random\nfrom typing import Dict, List, Optional, Tuple\n\nimport numpy as np\n\nfrom adapters.networkx_adapter import NetworkXAdapter\nfrom core.embedding_engine import EmbeddingEngine\n\nlogger = logging.getLogger(\"cerebrum.graph_bridge\")\n\n\nclass GraphBridgeEngine:\n    \"\"\"\n    Proactive cross-component bridge synthesizer.\n\n    See module docstring for full description.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        min_similarity:     float = 0.80,\n        top_k:              int   = 5,\n        max_degree:         int   = 2,\n        max_bridges:        int   = 500_000,\n        max_frontier_nodes: int   = 10_000,\n        batch_size:         int   = 512,\n        seed:               int   = 42,\n    ):\n        self.min_similarity     = min_similarity\n        self.top_k              = top_k\n        self.max_degree         = max_degree\n        self.max_bridges        = max_bridges\n        self.max_frontier_nodes = max_frontier_nodes\n        self.batch_size         = batch_size\n        self.seed               = seed\n\n    def describe(self) -> str:\n        return (\n            f\"GraphBridgeEngine: cross-component bridges via embedding similarity \"\n            f\"(min_sim={self.min_similarity}, top_k={self.top_k}, \"\n            f\"max_degree={self.max_degree}, max_bridges={self.max_bridges:,})\"\n        )\n\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n    # Main entry point\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    def apply(\n        self,\n        adapter:          NetworkXAdapter,\n        embedding_engine: EmbeddingEngine,\n    ) -> int:\n        \"\"\"\n        Apply the bridge engine to ``adapter`` using ``embedding_engine``\n        as a fixed semantic distance metric.\n\n        Returns the number of bridge edges added.\n        \"\"\"\n        import networkx as nx\n\n        G         = adapter._G\n        G_und     = G.to_undirected() if G.is_directed() else G\n        rng       = random.Random(self.seed)\n\n        # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n        # 1. Identify connected components and their sizes\n        # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n        logger.info(\"GraphBridgeEngine: computing connected components...\")\n        components = list(nx.connected_components(G_und))\n        n_comp     = len(components)\n        logger.info(\"  %d connected components\", n_comp)\n\n        if n_comp <= 1:\n            logger.info(\"  Graph is fully connected â€” no bridging needed.\")\n            return 0\n\n        # Build node â†’ component_id map\n        node_to_comp: Dict[str, int] = {}\n        for cid, members in enumerate(components):\n            for n in members:\n                node_to_comp[n] = cid\n\n        # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n        # 2. Select frontier nodes\n        #    Criteria: degree <= max_degree, from components of size 1..50\n        # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n        logger.info(\n            \"GraphBridgeEngine: selecting frontier nodes \"\n            \"(max_degree=%d, component size 1-50)...\",\n            self.max_degree,\n        )\n        frontier_nodes: List[str] = []\n        for cid, members in enumerate(components):\n            comp_size = len(members)\n            if comp_size > 50:\n                # Large components have enough internal connectivity â€” only\n                # add a small sample of their peripheral nodes\n                candidates = [\n                    n for n in members\n                    if G_und.degree(n) <= self.max_degree\n                ]\n                if candidates:\n                    sample_n = min(5, len(candidates))\n                    frontier_nodes.extend(rng.sample(candidates, sample_n))\n         
   else:\n                # Small components: include all low-degree nodes\n                for n in members:\n                    if G_und.degree(n) <= self.max_degree:\n                        frontier_nodes.append(n)\n\n        logger.info(\"  %d frontier nodes identified\", len(frontier_nodes))\n\n        if not frontier_nodes:\n            logger.info(\"  No frontier nodes found â€” skipping bridging.\")\n            return 0\n\n        # Sample down to max_frontier_nodes\n        if len(frontier_nodes) > self.max_frontier_nodes:\n            logger.info(\n                \"  Sampling %d frontier nodes from %d candidates\",\n                self.max_frontier_nodes, len(frontier_nodes),\n            )\n            frontier_nodes = rng.sample(frontier_nodes, self.max_frontier_nodes)\n\n        # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n        # 3. Encode frontier nodes with the fixed embedding engine\n        # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n        logger.info(\n            \"GraphBridgeEngine: encoding %d frontier nodes...\",\n            len(frontier_nodes),\n        )\n        entity_labels: Dict[str, str] = {}\n        for n in frontier_nodes:\n            data  = G.nodes[n] if n in G.nodes else {}\n            label = data.get(\"label\") or data.get(\"friendly_name\") or str(n)\n            entity_labels[n] = label\n\n        embeddings_map = embedding_engine.encode_entities(entity_labels)\n\n        # Build ordered array\n        valid_nodes = [n for n in frontier_nodes if n in embeddings_map]\n        if not valid_nodes:\n            logger.warning(\"GraphBridgeEngine: no embeddings produced â€” aborting.\")\n            return 0\n\n        emb_array = np.array(\n            [embeddings_map[n] for n in valid_nodes],\n            dtype=np.float32,\n        )\n\n        # L2-normalize for cosine similarity via dot product\n        norms = np.linalg.norm(emb_array, axis=1, keepdims=True)\n        norms = np.where(norms == 0, 1.0, norms)\n        emb_norm = emb_array / norms\n\n        logger.info(\n            \"GraphBridgeEngine: embedding matrix %s â€” computing similarities...\",\n            emb_norm.shape,\n        )\n\n        # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n        # 4. Batch cosine similarity + bridge selection\n        # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n        comp_ids = np.array([node_to_comp[n] for n in valid_nodes], dtype=np.int32)\n        n_nodes  = len(valid_nodes)\n\n        to_add:   List[Tuple[str, str, Dict]] = []\n        added_set: set = set()\n\n        # Process in batches (query side)\n        for batch_start in range(0, n_nodes, self.batch_size):\n            batch_end   = min(batch_start + self.batch_size, n_nodes)\n            batch_emb   = emb_norm[batch_start:batch_end]      # (B, D)\n            batch_comps = comp_ids[batch_start:batch_end]       # (B,)\n            batch_nodes = valid_nodes[batch_start:batch_end]\n\n            # Cosine similarities: (B, N)\n            sim_matrix = batch_emb @ emb_norm.T                 # (B, N)\n\n            for i, (src_node, src_comp) in enumerate(\n                zip(batch_nodes, batch_comps)\n            ):\n                sims     = sim_matrix[i]                        # (N,)\n                # Zero out same-component and self matches\n                mask     = comp_ids == src_comp\n                sims_filt = sims.copy()\n                sims_filt[mask] = 0.0\n\n                # Get top-k indices above threshold\n                top_idx = np.argpartition(sims_filt, -self.top_k)[-self.top_k:]\n                
top_idx = top_idx[sims_filt[top_idx] >= self.min_similarity]\n                if len(top_idx) == 0:\n           
         continue\n\n                # Sort descending by similarity\n                top_idx = top_idx[np.argsort(sims_filt[top_idx])[::-1]]\n\n                for j in top_idx:\n                    tgt_node = valid_nodes[j]\n                    tgt_comp = int(comp_ids[j])\n                    sim_val  = float(sims_filt[j])\n                    pair     = (\n                        (src_node, tgt_node)\n                        if src_node < tgt_node\n                        else (tgt_node, src_node)\n                    )\n                    if pair in added_set:\n                        continue\n                    if G.has_edge(src_node, tgt_node):\n         
               continue\n                    added_set.add(pair)\n                    prov = (\n                
        f\"rule:bridge_embed\"\n                        f\"|sim:{sim_val:.4f}\"\n                        f\"|from:{src_node}\"\n                        f\"|to:{tgt_node}\"\n                        f\"|components:{src_comp}->{tgt_comp}\"\n                    )\n                    # Add bidirectional edges for DiGraph so traversal can cross\n                    to_add.append((src_node, tgt_node, {\n                        \"relation\":   \"bridge:similar\",\n                        \"weight\":     sim_val,\n                        \"confidence\": sim_val,\n                        \"provenance\": prov,\n                        \"synthetic\":  True,\n                
    }))\n                    if G.is_directed():\n                        to_add.append((tgt_node, src_node, {\n                            \"relation\":   \"bridge:similar\",\n                            \"weight\":     sim_val,\n                            \"confidence\": sim_val,\n                            \"provenance\": prov,\n                            \"synthetic\":  True,\n                        }))\n\n                    if len(to_add) >= self.max_bridges:\n                        break\n\n                if len(to_add) >= self.max_bridges:\n                    logger.warning(\n                        \"GraphBridgeEngine: hit max_bridges cap %d\", self.max_bridges\n                    )\n                    break\n\n            if len(to_add) >= self.max_bridges:\n                break\n\n        # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n        # 5. Apply edges to graph\n        # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n        for src, tgt, attrs in to_add:\n            G.add_edge(src, tgt, **attrs)\n\n        new = len(to_add)\n        if new:\n            logger.info(\"GraphBridgeEngine: added %d bridge edges\", new)\n            if hasattr(adapter, \"_ngram_index\"):\n                del adapter._ngram_index\n\n        return new\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":"tool_use","stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":3,"cache_creation_input_tokens":46223,"cache_read_input_tokens":0,"output_tokens":3471,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":46223,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[],"speed":"standard"}},"requestId":"req_011CZdRpdk8zJbmZcGTXLGag","type":"assistant","uuid":"01d7310f-8f08-47e7-904a-20e860ea8f35","timestamp":"2026-04-01T17:16:26.624Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"E:\\Development\\Cerebrum","sessionId":"1b39a2ba-e177-466c-ade0-cb0f0629b3b0","version":"2.1.87","gitBranch":"main","slug":"dynamic-marinating-meadow"}

{"parentUuid":"01d7310f-8f08-47e7-904a-20e860ea8f35","isSidechain":false,"promptId":"c9ba211a-02f5-43be-be1c-e95669f345b4","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01KBKQDjenvWANeWBNWKhH3a","type":"tool_result","content":"File created successfully at: E:\\Development\\Cerebrum\\core\\graph_bridge.py"}]},"uuid":"1bdafa3c-9331-427f-8d79-42799d37ad22","timestamp":"2026-04-01T17:16:26.831Z","toolUseResult":{"type":"create","filePath":"E:\\Development\\Cerebrum\\core\\graph_bridge.py","content":"\"\"\"\nGraphBridgeEngine â€” proactive cross-component link synthesis for CEREBRUM.\n\nThis module implements the second layer of the Enhancement Layer:\n\n  THALAMUS â†’ [complete()] â†’ [enhance()] â†’ build() â†’ CORTEX\n\nProblem\n-------\nCWQ and similar KG benchmarks contain per-question 2-hop subgraphs that were\nextracted independently.  When merged into a single scaffold, entities that\nappear in *different* questions but refer to the same or semantically related\nconcepts end up in disconnected graph components.  DSCF therefore produces\nhundreds of thousands of isolated communities, and BeamTraversal cannot cross\ncomponent boundaries â€” resulting in near-zero multi-hop recall.\n\nSolution\n--------\nGraphBridgeEngine is a *proactive* bridge synthesizer that runs BEFORE\nBeamTraversal.  It:\n\n  1. Detects connected components in the graph.\n  2. Identifies \"frontier\" nodes â€” low-degree nodes on the periphery of small\n     components that are likely missing inter-component links.\n  3. Uses pre-trained SentenceEngine embeddings (fixed, not trained on task\n     data) to find pairs of frontier nodes in *different* components whose\n     cosine similarity exceeds ``min_similarity``.\n  4. Materializes a bridge edge with a glass-box provenance string that records\n     the similarity score, the source/target frontier nodes, and the component\n     IDs being connected.\n\nEvery synthetic edge is fully traceable:\n    provenance = \"rule:bridge_embed|sim:{s:.4f}|from:{F}|to:{N}|components:{c1}->{c2}\"\n\nThis is NOT link prediction in the ML sense â€” no model is trained on the task.\nSentenceEngine is used purely as a fixed semantic projector, identical to how\nit encodes entities during the standard build() pipeline.\n\nCrystal- vs Glass-box classification\n-------------------------------------\n  CompositionRule / GeneralizedCompositionRule = Crystal-box\n    (purely logical, no numerical thresholds, zero uncertainty)\n  GraphBridgeEngine = Glass-box\n    (transparent algorithm + confidence scores; uses a pre-trained, frozen\n    embedding model as a fixed distance metric â€” no task-specific training)\n\nParameters\n----------\nmin_similarity    : cosine similarity threshold for creating a bridge edge\n              
      (default 0.80 â€” empirically tuned for Freebase name strings)\ntop_k             : maximum bridges per frontier node (default 5)\nmax_degree        : maximum degree for a node to qualify as a frontier node\n          
          (default 2 â€” keeps only genuinely peripheral nodes)\nmax_bridges       : hard cap on total bridge edges added (default 500_000)\nmax_frontier_nodes: maximum frontier nodes to embed (default 10_000).\n            
        Nodes are sampled from components with 1â€“50 nodes.\nbatch_size        : embedding batch size (default 512)\n\nUsage\n-----\n    from core.graph_bridge import GraphBridgeEngine\n    bridge_engine = GraphBridgeEngine(min_similarity=0.82, top_k=3)\n    n_added = bridge_engine.apply(adapter, embedding_engine)\n\"\"\"\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport logging\nimport random\nfrom typing import Dict, List, Optional, Tuple\n\nimport numpy as np\n\nfrom adapters.networkx_adapter import NetworkXAdapter\nfrom core.embedding_engine import EmbeddingEngine\n\nlogger = logging.getLogger(\"cerebrum.graph_bridge\")\n\n\nclass GraphBridgeEngine:\n    \"\"\"\n    Proactive cross-component bridge synthesizer.\n\n    See module docstring for full description.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        min_similarity:     float = 0.80,\n        top_k:              int   = 5,\n        max_degree:         int   = 2,\n        max_bridges:        int   = 500_000,\n        max_frontier_nodes: int   = 10_000,\n        batch_size:         int   = 512,\n        seed:               int   = 42,\n    ):\n        self.min_similarity     = min_similarity\n        self.top_k              = top_k\n        self.max_degree         = max_degree\n        self.max_bridges        = max_bridges\n        self.max_frontier_nodes = max_frontier_nodes\n        self.batch_size         = batch_size\n        self.seed               = seed\n\n    def describe(self) -> str:\n        return (\n            f\"GraphBridgeEngine: cross-component bridges via embedding similarity \"\n            f\"(min_sim={self.min_similarity}, top_k={self.top_k}, \"\n            f\"max_degree={self.max_degree}, max_bridges={self.max_bridges:,})\"\n        )\n\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n    # Main entry point\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    def apply(\n        self,\n        adapter:          NetworkXAdapter,\n        embedding_engine: EmbeddingEngine,\n    ) -> int:\n        \"\"\"\n        Apply the bridge engine to ``adapter`` using ``embedding_engine``\n        as a fixed semantic distance metric.\n\n        Returns the number of bridge edges added.\n        \"\"\"\n        import networkx as nx\n\n        G         = adapter._G\n        G_und     = G.to_undirected() if G.is_directed() else G\n        rng       = random.Random(self.seed)\n\n        # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n        # 1. Identify connected components and their sizes\n        # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n        logger.info(\"GraphBridgeEngine: computing connected components...\")\n        components = list(nx.connected_components(G_und))\n        n_comp     = len(components)\n        logger.info(\"  %d connected components\", n_comp)\n\n        if n_comp <= 1:\n            logger.info(\"  Graph is fully connected â€” no bridging needed.\")\n            return 0\n\n        # Build node â†’ component_id map\n        node_to_comp: Dict[str, int] = {}\n        for cid, members in enumerate(components):\n            for n in members:\n                node_to_comp[n] = cid\n\n        # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n        # 2. Select frontier nodes\n        #    Criteria: degree <= max_degree, from components of size 1..50\n        # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n        logger.info(\n            \"GraphBridgeEngine: selecting frontier nodes \"\n            \"(max_degree=%d, component size 1-50)...\",\n            self.max_degree,\n        )\n        frontier_nodes: List[str] = []\n        for cid, members in enumerate(components):\n            comp_size = len(members)\n            if comp_size > 50:\n                # Large components have enough internal connectivity â€” only\n                # add a small sample of their peripheral nodes\n                candidates = [\n                    n for n in members\n                    if G_und.degree(n) <= self.max_degree\n                
]\n                if candidates:\n                    sample_n = min(5, len(candidates))\n                    frontier_nodes.extend(rng.sample(candidates, sample_n))\n            else:\n                # Small components: include all low-degree nodes\n                for n in members:\n                    if G_und.degree(n) <= self.max_degree:\n                        frontier_nodes.append(n)\n\n        logger.info(\"  %d frontier nodes identified\", len(frontier_nodes))\n\n        if not frontier_nodes:\n            logger.info(\"  No frontier nodes found â€” skipping bridging.\")\n            return 0\n\n        # Sample down to max_frontier_nodes\n        if len(frontier_nodes) > self.max_frontier_nodes:\n            logger.info(\n                \"  Sampling %d frontier nodes from %d candidates\",\n                self.max_frontier_nodes, len(frontier_nodes),\n            )\n            frontier_nodes = rng.sample(frontier_nodes, self.max_frontier_nodes)\n\n        # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n        # 3. Encode frontier nodes with the fixed embedding engine\n        # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n        logger.info(\n         
   \"GraphBridgeEngine: encoding %d frontier nodes...\",\n            len(frontier_nodes),\n        )\n        entity_labels: Dict[str, str] = {}\n        for n in frontier_nodes:\n            data  = G.nodes[n] if n in G.nodes else {}\n            label = data.get(\"label\") or data.get(\"friendly_name\") or str(n)\n            entity_labels[n] = label\n\n        embeddings_map = embedding_engine.encode_entities(entity_labels)\n\n        # Build ordered array\n        valid_nodes = [n for n in frontier_nodes if n in embeddings_map]\n        if not valid_nodes:\n            logger.warning(\"GraphBridgeEngine: no embeddings produced â€” aborting.\")\n            return 0\n\n        emb_array = np.array(\n            [embeddings_map[n] for n in valid_nodes],\n            dtype=np.float32,\n        )\n\n        # L2-normalize for cosine similarity via dot product\n        norms = np.linalg.norm(emb_array, axis=1, keepdims=True)\n        norms = np.where(norms == 0, 1.0, norms)\n        emb_norm = emb_array / norms\n\n        logger.info(\n            \"GraphBridgeEngine: embedding matrix %s â€” computing similarities...\",\n            emb_norm.shape,\n        )\n\n        # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n        # 4. Batch cosine similarity + bridge selection\n        # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n        comp_ids = np.array([node_to_comp[n] for n in valid_nodes], dtype=np.int32)\n        n_nodes  = len(valid_nodes)\n\n        to_add:   List[Tuple[str, str, Dict]] = []\n        added_set: set = set()\n\n        # Process in batches (query side)\n        for batch_start in range(0, n_nodes, self.batch_size):\n            batch_end   = min(batch_start + self.batch_size, n_nodes)\n          
  batch_emb   = emb_norm[batch_start:batch_end]      # (B, D)\n            batch_comps = comp_ids[batch_start:batch_end]       # (B,)\n            batch_nodes = valid_nodes[batch_start:batch_end]\n\n            # Cosine similarities: (B, N)\n            sim_matrix = batch_emb @ emb_norm.T                 # (B, N)\n\n            for i, (src_node, src_comp) in enumerate(\n                zip(batch_nodes, batch_comps)\n            ):\n            
    sims     = sim_matrix[i]                        # (N,)\n                # Zero out same-component and self matches\n                mask     = comp_ids == src_comp\n                sims_filt = sims.copy()\n              
  sims_filt[mask] = 0.0\n\n                # Get top-k indices above threshold\n                top_idx = np.argpartition(sims_filt, -self.top_k)[-self.top_k:]\n                top_idx = top_idx[sims_filt[top_idx] >= self.min_similarity]\n                if len(top_idx) == 0:\n                    continue\n\n                # Sort descending by similarity\n                top_idx = top_idx[np.argsort(sims_filt[top_idx])[::-1]]\n\n              
  for j in top_idx:\n                    tgt_node = valid_nodes[j]\n                    tgt_comp = int(comp_ids[j])\n                    sim_val  = float(sims_filt[j])\n                    pair     = (\n                     
   (src_node, tgt_node)\n                        if src_node < tgt_node\n                        else (tgt_node, src_node)\n                    )\n                    if pair in added_set:\n                        continue\n                    if G.has_edge(src_node, tgt_node):\n                        continue\n                    added_set.add(pair)\n                    prov = (\n                        f\"rule:bridge_embed\"\n               
         f\"|sim:{sim_val:.4f}\"\n                        f\"|from:{src_node}\"\n                        f\"|to:{tgt_node}\"\n                        f\"|components:{src_comp}->{tgt_comp}\"\n                    )\n          
          # Add bidirectional edges for DiGraph so traversal can cross\n                    to_add.append((src_node, tgt_node, {\n                        \"relation\":   \"bridge:similar\",\n                        \"weight\":     sim_val,\n                        \"confidence\": sim_val,\n                        \"provenance\": prov,\n                        \"synthetic\":  True,\n                    }))\n                    if G.is_directed():\n                        to_add.append((tgt_node, src_node, {\n                            \"relation\":   \"bridge:similar\",\n                            \"weight\":     sim_val,\n                            \"confidence\": sim_val,\n                            \"provenance\": prov,\n                            \"synthetic\":  True,\n                        }))\n\n                    if len(to_add) >= self.max_bridges:\n                    
    break\n\n                if len(to_add) >= self.max_bridges:\n                    logger.warning(\n         
               \"GraphBridgeEngine: hit max_bridges cap %d\", self.max_bridges\n                    )\n         
           break\n\n            if len(to_add) >= self.max_bridges:\n                break\n\n        # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n        # 5. Apply edges to graph\n        # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n        for src, tgt, attrs in to_add:\n           
 G.add_edge(src, tgt, **attrs)\n\n        new = len(to_add)\n        if new:\n            logger.info(\"GraphBridgeEngine: added %d bridge edges\", new)\n            if hasattr(adapter, \"_ngram_index\"):\n                del adapter._ngram_index\n\n        return new\n","structuredPatch":[],"originalFile":null},"sourceToolAssistantUUID":"01d7310f-8f08-47e7-904a-20e860ea8f35","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"E:\\Development\\Cerebrum","sessionId":"1b39a2ba-e177-466c-ade0-cb0f0629b3b0","version":"2.1.87","gitBranch":"main","slug":"dynamic-marinating-meadow"}
